title: Moonweave generated canonical Concept payload schemas

CommandOutputObservation.title: command output observation payload
CommandOutputObservation.description: records observable command output as context-ingress evidence without owning the command execution or container that produced it.

observation_id.title: observation ID
observation_id.description: Stable identifier for one observation of an execution result.

observed_at.title: observed at
observed_at.description: UTC instant at which the observation record was formed.

ContextIngressEvent.title: context ingress event payload
ContextIngressEvent.description: observable event in which a message, instruction, source span, retrieved candidate, tool observation, execution output, or summarized artifact becomes available to a model call or agent step.

EnvironmentBindingReference.title: environment binding reference payload
EnvironmentBindingReference.description: references a runtime environment binding relevant to observed execution output without exposing hidden or secret values by default.

ExecutionResultReference.title: execution result reference payload
ExecutionResultReference.description: references a runtime or tool execution result whose observable output or status is being staged into context.

ExitStatusObservation.title: exit status observation payload
ExitStatusObservation.description: represents an exit status after it has been observed and staged as context evidence for an agent step.

observation_id.title: observation ID
observation_id.description: Stable identifier for one observation of an execution result.

observed_at.title: observed at
observed_at.description: UTC instant at which the observation record was formed.

exit_status.title: command exit status
exit_status.description: An exit code or termination status is a scalar observed value of ExitStatusObservation without independent business identity; a field can still record code, signal, and reason.

StandardError.title: standard error stream payload
StandardError.description: captures the stderr stream emitted by a command, process, sandbox, or tool execution as observable diagnostic output.

StandardErrorChunk.title: standard error chunk payload
StandardErrorChunk.description: represents a bounded stderr segment with ordering, diagnostic meaning, source span, truncation, and context-selection metadata.

StandardOutput.title: standard output stream payload
StandardOutput.description: captures the stdout stream emitted by a command, process, sandbox, or tool execution as observable output available for staging into context.

StandardOutputChunk.title: standard output chunk payload
StandardOutputChunk.description: represents a bounded stdout segment with ordering, source span, truncation, and context-selection metadata.

WorkingDirectoryReference.title: working directory reference payload
WorkingDirectoryReference.description: references the working directory relevant to observed execution output without making the directory itself a context-ingress artifact.

ExecutionObservation.title: execution observation payload
ExecutionObservation.description: ExecutionObservation is identity-bearing information with observation time, a reference to the observed execution, visible payload, and source evidence. It records permitted observable execution phenomena without owning the command or execution activity.

observation_id.title: observation ID
observation_id.description: Stable identifier for one observation of an execution result.

observed_at.title: observed at
observed_at.description: UTC instant at which the observation record was formed.

ExecutionReference.title: execution reference payload
ExecutionReference.description: ExecutionReference is information with a reference identifier, reference kind, target system, and visibility constraints. It points to execution-related objects without copying them or exposing hidden values.

AudioBlock.title: audio block payload
AudioBlock.description: represents audio content or transcribed audio observations staged into context with modality and source metadata.

encoding.title: encoding
encoding.description: Character or binary encoding used to interpret the content bytes.

modality.title: message modality
modality.description: Modality is a controlled classification dimension of ContentBlock; concrete text, image, and audio values are expressed by the field together with strict block subtypes, not a shadow node.

mime_type.title: media type
mime_type.description: Internet media type of the content; it is a field rather than a graph node.

token_count.title: token count
token_count.description: Token count is a numeric measurement relative to a tokenizer; as a ContentBlock field it can carry count and tokenizer version without becoming a graph node.

block_id.title: content-block ID
block_id.description: Stable identifier for the block within its containing message or content package.

CodeBlock.description: represents source code, patches, stack traces, shell snippets, or executable text staged into context.

CandidateRank.title: candidate rank observation payload
CandidateRank.description: Candidate rank is an observed quality of ordinal position in a particular discovery run, not a subtype of index, result, or candidate; it remains a concept to carry method and evidence.

DiscoverySurface.title: discovery surface payload
DiscoverySurface.description: identifies a lightweight surface such as search, file listing, memory namespace, tool descriptor list, or protocol resource list used to discover context candidates.

IndexPointer.title: index pointer payload
IndexPointer.description: points from a context selection process to an index entry, search surface, or retrieval handle that may produce candidate content.

IndexVersionReference.title: index version reference payload
IndexVersionReference.description: references the index version, snapshot, build, or configuration that produced a discovery candidate.

LightIndex.title: lightweight discovery index payload
LightIndex.description: represents a lightweight discovery surface or pointer set used to find candidate context without owning the full indexing system.

LightweightRetrievalTrace.title: lightweight retrieval trace payload
LightweightRetrievalTrace.description: records observable query, candidate, score, rank, and source-reference evidence for a lightweight context discovery pass.

RetrievalScore.title: retrieval score payload
RetrievalScore.description: records relevance, confidence, similarity, lexical match, or rerank evidence attached to a retrieved context candidate.

RetrievedCandidate.title: retrieved candidate payload
RetrievedCandidate.description: represents content returned by retrieval before the system decides whether it enters the context package.

rank.title: rank
rank.description: One-based ordering position of the result within one query's candidates.

result_id.title: discovery-result ID
result_id.description: Stable identifier for one lightweight discovery result.

RetrievedContextCandidate.title: retrieved context candidate payload
RetrievedContextCandidate.description: represents a retrieved candidate evaluated specifically for inclusion in a model-call or agent-step context package.

rank.title: rank
rank.description: One-based ordering position of the result within one query's candidates.

result_id.title: discovery-result ID
result_id.description: Stable identifier for one lightweight discovery result.

SearchResult.title: search result payload
SearchResult.description: records a candidate returned by a search or discovery surface, including source reference, score, rank, and selection status.

rank.title: rank
rank.description: One-based ordering position of the result within one query's candidates.

result_id.title: discovery-result ID
result_id.description: Stable identifier for one lightweight discovery result.

SearchScore.title: search score payload
SearchScore.description: records the score attached to a retrieved candidate, search result, index hit, or source pointer during context discovery.

DiscoveryIndex.title: discovery index payload
DiscoveryIndex.description: DiscoveryIndex is an identity-bearing, queryable information artifact with build or refresh time, coverage, and version reference for finding context candidates. It does not own Memory's persistent-index storage, shards, or lifecycle.

DiscoveryResult.title: discovery result payload
DiscoveryResult.description: DiscoveryResult is information with result identity, a reference to the producing query or surface, source reference, optional score, and selection status. Being a result does not imply inclusion in a ContextPackage.

rank.title: rank
rank.description: One-based ordering position of the result within one query's candidates.

result_id.title: discovery-result ID
result_id.description: Stable identifier for one lightweight discovery result.

DiscoveryScore.title: discovery score payload
DiscoveryScore.description: DiscoveryScore is an observed quality of a discovery result on relevance, similarity, lexical-match, or reranking scales. It records method, scale, direction, time, and scored subject; it is neither a result nor an index.

AssistantMessage.title: assistant message payload
AssistantMessage.description: records an assistant-authored message envelope that may contain model output, tool-call intent, citations, or disclosed reasoning summaries.

receiver_id.title: message receiver
receiver_id.description: The receiver is a Message reference field to an Actor, model call, or tool; a role occurrence should not be an independent type node.

message_role.title: message role
message_role.description: System, developer, user, assistant, and tool are controlled Message classification values; stable genera are already expressed by Message subtypes, so no shadow role node is retained.

sender_id.title: message sender
sender_id.description: The sender is a Message reference field to an Actor, service, tool, or user, not a reusable subject type.

reply_to_id.title: reply-to message identifier
reply_to_id.description: A message may reference the stable identifier of the message it replies to. This relational scalar has no independent ontological identity, so it becomes Message.reply_to_id while the Message --replies_to→ Message type relation declares its endpoint semantics.

message_id.title: message ID
message_id.description: Stable identifier for the message within its protocol or conversation scope.

conversation_id.title: conversation ID
conversation_id.description: Identifier value correlating the containing conversation, not a separate Concept node.

sequence_index.title: sequence index
sequence_index.description: Protocol-defined or zero-based position of the message in its conversation order.

correlation_id.title: correlation ID
correlation_id.description: Optional identifier correlating requests, responses, and related events.

ContextPackage.title: context package payload
ContextPackage.description: bundles the exact message envelopes, instructions, content blocks, source references, retrieval candidates, tool observations, citations, exclusions, and budget metadata visible to one model call or agent step.

Conversation.title: conversation payload
Conversation.description: groups ordered message turns, instructions, tool observations, and context packages that share a conversation or thread identity.

conversation_id.title: conversation identifier
conversation_id.description: A conversation identifier is an identity key of Conversation with no independent ontological lifecycle, so it becomes a required structural field.

thread_id.title: thread identifier
thread_id.description: A thread identifier is a Conversation correlation key for resumption or task grouping and has no independent ontological identity, so it becomes a field.

ConversationTurn.title: conversation turn payload
ConversationTurn.description: identifies one ordered exchange position in a conversation and binds message content to turn index, role, sender, and reply context.

turn_index.title: turn index
turn_index.description: Turn index is an ordinal value on ConversationTurn, not an index-type concept; ordering is additionally validated by precedes.

DeveloperMessage.title: developer message payload
DeveloperMessage.description: records a developer-authored message envelope that supplies implementation guidance, policy, constraints, or task framing below system authority and above ordinary user content.

FewShotExample.description: stores an example input-output or demonstration pattern used to condition behavior while remaining traceable as context content.

Instruction.title: instruction payload
Instruction.description: records a directive that constrains agent behavior and carries authority, scope, priority, provenance, and conflict-resolution metadata.

priority.title: Priority
priority.description: captures priority as a integer scalar on agent-system artifacts, resources, events, policies, or adapter records.

InstructionApplicability.title: instruction applicability payload
InstructionApplicability.description: states the conditions under which an instruction is active, inactive, inherited, or limited for a context package or agent step.

InstructionAuthority.title: instruction authority payload
InstructionAuthority.description: states the authority level and source of an instruction so conflicts among system, developer, user, policy, retrieved, or tool-originated directives can be resolved.

InstructionOverride.title: instruction override payload
InstructionOverride.description: records an authorized replacement or narrowing of one instruction by another with higher authority or more specific scope.

priority.title: Priority
priority.description: captures priority as a integer scalar on agent-system artifacts, resources, events, policies, or adapter records.

InstructionPriority.title: instruction priority payload
InstructionPriority.description: records the precedence ordering used when multiple applicable instructions compete for control of a model call or agent step.

InstructionProvenance.title: instruction provenance payload
InstructionProvenance.description: links an instruction to its source message, policy, prompt template, retrieved artifact, protocol envelope, or human approval record.

InstructionResolution.title: instruction resolution payload
InstructionResolution.description: records the selected outcome for an instruction conflict, including which directive was followed, overridden, ignored, narrowed, or escalated.

InstructionScope.title: instruction scope payload
InstructionScope.description: bounds where an instruction applies, such as session, task, tool call, retrieved document, output format, or trust boundary.

ProtocolEnvelope.description: captures protocol-level wrapping around a message, including task identity, sender, receiver, artifact references, and capability metadata without promoting adapter-specific fields into core.

ConversationElement.title: conversation structure element payload
ConversationElement.description: ConversationElement is identity-bearing information participating in conversation structure. Its subtypes cover the full conversation, an ordered turn, and retained history; it does not prescribe sender role or message payload.

InstructionMetadata.title: instruction metadata payload
InstructionMetadata.description: InstructionMetadata is information associated with one or more Instructions and carrying its own provenance and validity time. It explains when, why, for whom, and at what precedence an instruction applies; it is not the directive content itself.

ContextSelectionDecision.title: context selection decision payload
ContextSelectionDecision.description: This is decision information recording why a candidate was included, deferred, suppressed, or excluded, rather than treating the decision record as a generic event type.

DisclosedOutputSegment.title: disclosed output segment payload
DisclosedOutputSegment.description: represents an output segment that has been made visible to a recipient or downstream context together with citation and boundary metadata.

DisclosureStage.title: disclosure stage activity payload
DisclosureStage.description: records a step in which output or context content is revealed, withheld, escalated, redacted, or released across a boundary.

HeadTailWindow.title: head-tail output window payload
HeadTailWindow.description: retains the beginning and ending spans of long output so important setup and terminal evidence can remain visible under length limits.

length_limit.title: length limit
length_limit.description: Maximum characters, bytes, or tokens admitted by the window, interpreted by unit.

unit.title: unit
unit.description: Controlled measurement unit used by length_limit.

OutputChunk.title: output chunk payload
OutputChunk.description: represents a bounded segment of command, tool, model, process, or artifact output prepared for citation, truncation, disclosure, or later context staging.

OutputCitation.title: output citation payload
OutputCitation.description: links visible output or a disclosed segment to the source span, retrieval candidate, trace event, or artifact that supports it.

OutputSegment.title: output segment payload
OutputSegment.description: records an addressable segment within an output stream with ordering, source, visibility, and optional citation metadata.

OutputStream.title: output stream payload
OutputStream.description: records an ordered stream of output segments produced by a model, command, process, or tool before windowing or disclosure.

OutputWindow.title: output window payload
OutputWindow.description: identifies the visible or retained range of output that may be shown, cited, summarized, or passed into later context.

length_limit.title: length limit
length_limit.description: Maximum characters, bytes, or tokens admitted by the window, interpreted by unit.

unit.title: unit
unit.description: Controlled measurement unit used by length_limit.

SuppressedContext.title: suppressed context payload
SuppressedContext.description: records content intentionally kept out of visible context because of policy, trust boundary, relevance, privacy, or budget constraints.

TruncatedContextSpan.title: truncated context span payload
TruncatedContextSpan.description: records the portion of content removed or shortened by a context window, length limit, summarization, or output clipping decision.

VisibleContextWindow.title: visible context window payload
VisibleContextWindow.description: represents the portion of content visible to a specific actor, model call, or agent step after selection, truncation, suppression, and disclosure controls.

length_limit.title: length limit
length_limit.description: Maximum characters, bytes, or tokens admitted by the window, interpreted by unit.

unit.title: unit
unit.description: Controlled measurement unit used by length_limit.

OutputInformation.title: output information payload
OutputInformation.description: OutputInformation is the root for output-related information with source, producer, order or extent, and visibility. Subtypes may be stream collections, addressable segments, visible-range specifications, or evidence citations, but not the activity producing output.

DisclosureArtifact.title: disclosure audit artifact payload
DisclosureArtifact.description: DisclosureArtifact is identity-bearing information with the producing selection or disclosure activity, recipient, boundary, policy basis, and handled-content reference. It audits disclosure outcomes and is not the disclosure policy or activity.

AccessPath.title: access path payload
AccessPath.description: describes the path or handle through which a source reference can be retrieved, subject to permission, policy, and trust boundary controls.

CitationAnchor.title: citation anchor payload
CitationAnchor.description: binds a visible citation to the exact source reference and span that support a message, output, summary, or artifact claim.

ContentDigest.title: content digest payload
ContentDigest.description: records a compact fingerprint of content used for deduplication, integrity checks, cache validation, or citation stability.

DatabaseRowReference.title: database row reference payload
DatabaseRowReference.description: references a specific database row, query result, or table slice that can support context content without owning the database.

uri.title: Resource URI
uri.description: captures URI as a string scalar on agent-system artifacts, resources, events, policies, or adapter records.

reference_id.title: reference ID
reference_id.description: Stable identifier for this reference record, not for the referent itself.

canonical_uri.title: canonical URI
canonical_uri.description: Canonical URI locating the referenced resource when available.

DataZoneReference.title: data zone reference payload
DataZoneReference.description: points from a context artifact to the data zone whose visibility, retention, and handling policy governs that artifact.

FileResourceReference.title: file resource reference payload
FileResourceReference.description: references a file-like resource by path, URI, version, digest, or span without modeling the full file system as context input.

uri.title: Resource URI
uri.description: captures URI as a string scalar on agent-system artifacts, resources, events, policies, or adapter records.

reference_id.title: reference ID
reference_id.description: Stable identifier for this reference record, not for the referent itself.

canonical_uri.title: canonical URI
canonical_uri.description: Canonical URI locating the referenced resource when available.

GraphNodeReference.title: graph node reference payload
GraphNodeReference.description: references a graph node or graph-local identifier that can support context content without owning the graph store.

uri.title: Resource URI
uri.description: captures URI as a string scalar on agent-system artifacts, resources, events, policies, or adapter records.

reference_id.title: reference ID
reference_id.description: Stable identifier for this reference record, not for the referent itself.

canonical_uri.title: canonical URI
canonical_uri.description: Canonical URI locating the referenced resource when available.

NetworkSourceReference.title: network source reference payload
NetworkSourceReference.description: references network-originated content or an endpoint-derived source while preserving trust-boundary and access-path metadata.

uri.title: Resource URI
uri.description: captures URI as a string scalar on agent-system artifacts, resources, events, policies, or adapter records.

reference_id.title: reference ID
reference_id.description: Stable identifier for this reference record, not for the referent itself.

canonical_uri.title: canonical URI
canonical_uri.description: Canonical URI locating the referenced resource when available.

ResourceDescriptor.title: resource descriptor payload
ResourceDescriptor.description: summarizes identity, type, provenance, location, access hints, and trust metadata for a source or resource reference staged into context.

SourceAnchor.title: source anchor payload
SourceAnchor.description: provides a stable anchor that can re-identify a source span after chunking, rendering, indexing, or document revision.

SourceCellRange.title: source cell range payload
SourceCellRange.description: records table, sheet, notebook, or matrix cell ranges that support context content or citations.

SourceChecksum.title: source checksum payload
SourceChecksum.description: records a digest or checksum used to detect whether cited source material has changed since ingestion or context staging.

SourceLineRange.title: source line range payload
SourceLineRange.description: records line-based source span boundaries used for code, logs, terminal output, documents, or text files.

SourceLocation.title: source location payload
SourceLocation.description: records a source position such as URI, file path, database locator, graph identifier, page, line, offset, or cell reference.

SourceOffset.title: source offset payload
SourceOffset.description: records byte, character, token, or structural offsets used to locate a source span precisely.

SourcePageRange.title: source page range payload
SourcePageRange.description: records page-based source span boundaries used for paginated documents, PDFs, or rendered reports.

SourceProvenance.title: source provenance payload
SourceProvenance.description: links a source reference to acquisition, ingestion, transformation, trust boundary, and evidence metadata.

SourceReference.title: source reference payload
SourceReference.description: identifies a cited or retrievable source object by stable reference without claiming ownership of the storage system that hosts it.

uri.title: Resource URI
uri.description: captures URI as a string scalar on agent-system artifacts, resources, events, policies, or adapter records.

reference_id.title: reference ID
reference_id.description: Stable identifier for this reference record, not for the referent itself.

canonical_uri.title: canonical URI
canonical_uri.description: Canonical URI locating the referenced resource when available.

SourceSpan.title: source span payload
SourceSpan.description: identifies the precise portion of a source that supports a context block, retrieval candidate, citation, summary, or disclosed output segment.

SourceVersion.title: source version payload
SourceVersion.description: records the version, revision, timestamp, commit, snapshot, or release identifier associated with a source reference.

TextDocument.title: text document payload
TextDocument.description: represents a text-bearing information source that can be referenced, chunked, indexed, cited, or assembled into context with source provenance.

TextDocumentReference.title: text document reference payload
TextDocumentReference.description: references a text document as source evidence while keeping the document store and ingestion system outside the context-ingress owner domain.

uri.title: Resource URI
uri.description: captures URI as a string scalar on agent-system artifacts, resources, events, policies, or adapter records.

reference_id.title: reference ID
reference_id.description: Stable identifier for this reference record, not for the referent itself.

canonical_uri.title: canonical URI
canonical_uri.description: Canonical URI locating the referenced resource when available.

TrustBoundaryReference.title: trust boundary reference payload
TrustBoundaryReference.description: points from a context artifact to the trust boundary crossed or relied on by its source, transport, retrieval, or disclosure path.

SourceIdentity.title: source identity information payload
SourceIdentity.description: SourceIdentity is information associated with a SourceReference to identify a concrete source state when location remains the same but content changes. It may use version identifiers, content digests, or checksums and must state the algorithm or identifier scheme.

AgentAsToolInvocation.title: agent as tool invocation payload
AgentAsToolInvocation.description: Agent-as-tool invocation is a DelegationProcess distinguished by the manager retaining control and answer ownership.

AnswerOwnership.title: answer ownership payload
AnswerOwnership.description: records which coordinating actor is accountable for the final response or artifact after delegation, handoff, review, or synthesis.

ContextIsolation.title: context isolation payload
ContextIsolation.description: bounds the messages, memory, tools, and source material visible to a worker or subagent during delegated work.

ControlOwnership.title: control ownership payload
ControlOwnership.description: records which actor currently controls the next workflow step, including whether control is retained by a manager or transferred through handoff.

DelegatedAuthority.title: delegated authority payload
DelegatedAuthority.description: states the permissions, tools, memory surfaces, data zones, and operations a delegate may use on behalf of the orchestrator.

DelegationBudget.title: delegation budget payload
DelegationBudget.description: sets token, time, cost, retry, tool-call, or context limits that constrain delegated work and routing choices.

DelegationContract.title: delegation contract payload
DelegationContract.description: specifies delegated work, expected result, authority scope, visible context, budget, completion criteria, and ownership boundaries between delegator and delegatee.

delegation_id.title: delegation ID
delegation_id.description: Stable identifier for one delegation contract among delegator, delegatee, and work scope.

status.title: delegation status
status.description: State in the proposed, accepted, active, completed, or revoked delegation lifecycle.

created_at.title: created at
created_at.description: UTC instant at which the delegation contract was formed.

delegator_id.title: delegator ID
delegator_id.description: Identifies the actor issuing the delegation and bearing authorization responsibility.

delegatee_id.title: delegatee ID
delegatee_id.description: Identifies the actor accepting the delegation and responsible for performance.

task_id.title: task ID
task_id.description: Pins the task covered by this delegation.

budget_id.title: budget ID
budget_id.description: Identifies the time, cost, or tool budget limiting delegated performance.

DelegationEvent.title: delegation event payload
DelegationEvent.description: observable event that initiates, updates, cancels, or completes delegated work under a delegation contract.

DelegationOwnership.title: delegation ownership payload
DelegationOwnership.description: records the responsibility split between delegator and delegatee, including retained answer ownership, transferred control, authority scope, and result accountability.

DelegationResult.title: delegation result payload
DelegationResult.description: observable result returned by a worker, subagent, tool-like agent, or remote delegate, including artifacts, status, warnings, and provenance.

Handoff.title: handoff payload
Handoff.description: control-transfer event that moves responsibility for a task, conversation, route, or runtime state to another agent actor or remote delegate.

HandoffTarget.title: handoff target payload
HandoffTarget.description: actor, remote agent, route, or protocol endpoint that receives control or responsibility after a handoff.

Orchestrator.title: orchestrator role payload
Orchestrator.description: Orchestrator denotes decomposition, delegation, routing, and synthesis responsibilities and is therefore a CoordinationRole borne by actors, not a fixed actor subtype.

ResponsibilityTransfer.title: responsibility transfer payload
ResponsibilityTransfer.description: records which responsibility changes owner during delegation or handoff, including task custody, control custody, answer ownership, and audit accountability.

SubagentContext.title: subagent context payload
SubagentContext.description: scoped context package visible to a subagent, including allowed messages, sources, memory, task state, tool results, and exclusions.

SubagentRole.title: subagent role payload
SubagentRole.description: SubagentRole is a CoordinationRole distinguished by bounded work, authority, context, and upward reporting.

TaskDistribution.title: task distribution payload
TaskDistribution.description: TaskDistribution is a DelegationProcess distinguished by assigning work items to selected performers with scope evidence.

WorkerAgent.title: worker role payload
WorkerAgent.description: Worker denotes responsibility for receiving scoped work and returning evidence and can be borne by an agent, model service, or tool service, so it is retyped as a CoordinationRole.

WorkerAvailability.title: worker availability payload
WorkerAvailability.description: records whether a worker or subagent can accept work, including load, lifecycle state, capability readiness, and fallback status.

WorkerCapabilityMatch.title: worker capability match payload
WorkerCapabilityMatch.description: records the evidence that a worker, subagent, model, or tool matches the required task capability, modality, authority, or domain.

WorkerPool.title: worker pool payload
WorkerPool.description: candidate set of workers, subagents, models, tools, or remote delegates available for selection by capability, load, cost, authority, and trust boundary.

WorkerSelection.title: worker selection payload
WorkerSelection.description: decision event that chooses a worker, subagent, model, tool, or route from a candidate pool using capability, availability, cost, safety, and context-fit evidence.

CoordinationRole.title: coordination role payload
CoordinationRole.description: A CoordinationRole specifies decomposition, delegation, performance, supervision, synthesis, or reporting responsibilities in orchestration and is borne by an actor through an ActorRoleBinding.

DelegationProcess.title: delegation process payload
DelegationProcess.description: A DelegationProcess is a coordination activity in which a delegator assigns scoped work, authority, context, and budget under a contract to a delegate role and receives a traceable result with explicit retained or transferred ownership.

CoordinationOwnershipRecord.title: coordination ownership record payload
CoordinationOwnershipRecord.description: A CoordinationOwnershipRecord identifies, at a time during delegation, handoff, or synthesis, which actor owns answer accountability, next-step control, or delegation responsibility, with scope, validity, and transfer provenance.

AggregationRule.title: aggregation rule payload
AggregationRule.description: composition rule that governs how parallel outputs, votes, section results, or evidence fragments are merged into a synthesis.

ChainStage.title: chain stage payload
ChainStage.description: one ordered stage in a prompt chain with explicit input, output, dependency, guard, and handoff or routing context.

OrchestrationTopology.title: orchestration topology payload
OrchestrationTopology.description: describes the coordination shape of a workflow, such as centralized manager-worker, hierarchical crew, graph route, parallel branch, or dynamic swarm.

topology_id.title: topology ID
topology_id.description: Stable identifier for the static orchestration topology across revisions.

version.title: topology version
version.description: Identifies one immutable revision of the topology specification.

failure_policy.title: failure policy
failure_policy.description: States how the topology stops, retries, or degrades when a part fails.

ParallelBranch.title: parallel branch payload
ParallelBranch.description: one branch in a parallelized workflow with its own assigned work, context slice, output, and merge expectations.

Parallelization.title: parallelization payload
Parallelization.description: Parallelization is a CompositionPattern distinguished by concurrent branch execution and fan-in.

PromptChain.title: prompt chain payload
PromptChain.description: PromptChain is a CompositionPattern distinguished by an ordered sequence of prompt or model-call stages.

SectionAssignment.title: section assignment payload
SectionAssignment.description: assignment record connecting a section of work to a worker, context slice, expected output, and synthesis role.

Sectioning.title: sectioning payload
Sectioning.description: Sectioning is a CompositionPattern distinguished by dividing larger work or artifacts into independently handled sections.

Synthesis.title: synthesis payload
Synthesis.description: Its definition is an observable process that combines inputs and produces a result, so it is retyped as an activity rather than mechanically placed under CompositionPattern.

SynthesisInput.title: synthesis input payload
SynthesisInput.description: worker output, retrieved evidence, tool result, vote, review signal, or artifact fragment consumed by a synthesis step.

SynthesisOutput.title: synthesis output payload
SynthesisOutput.description: coherent artifact, answer, plan, summary, or decision produced by a synthesis step with provenance back to its inputs.

SynthesisPlan.title: synthesis plan payload
SynthesisPlan.description: plan describing which inputs, votes, sections, evidence, and precedence rules a synthesis step must combine.

VoteBallot.title: vote ballot payload
VoteBallot.description: record of a worker, evaluator, model, or rule casting a choice, score, preference, or rationale summary in a voting workflow.

Voting.title: voting payload
Voting.description: Voting is a CompositionPattern distinguished by collecting multiple judgments and selecting or aggregating them under a rule.

CompositionPattern.title: composition pattern payload
CompositionPattern.description: A CompositionPattern is a static specification of stage or branch organization, sequential or concurrent constraints, input/output interfaces, fan-in, and failure policy that an orchestration topology can use.

CompositionPart.title: composition part payload
CompositionPart.description: A CompositionPart is a structural specification belonging to an orchestration topology or pattern with local inputs/outputs, sequential or concurrent position, assignment scope, and fan-in responsibility.

CritiqueArtifact.title: critique artifact payload
CritiqueArtifact.description: observable critique result produced by a review or evaluator step, including findings, requested changes, confidence, and provenance.

EvaluatorOptimizer.title: evaluator optimizer payload
EvaluatorOptimizer.description: EvaluatorOptimizer denotes responsibility for reviewing output and choosing revision, retry, escalation, or stop; it is a role, not a loop or fixed actor subtype.

FeedbackRouting.title: feedback routing payload
FeedbackRouting.description: control decision that sends feedback to a task, worker, route, revision plan, gate, or optimizer loop.

ImprovementAttempt.title: improvement attempt payload
ImprovementAttempt.description: bounded retry or revision attempt launched from feedback, review, gate outcome, or stop-retry lineage.

OptimizerState.title: optimizer state payload
OptimizerState.description: runtime-visible state of an evaluator-optimizer loop, including current attempt, selected feedback, budget, stopping context, and candidate quality.

ReflectionRecord.title: reflection record payload
ReflectionRecord.description: observable review summary or rationale artifact used for improvement while excluding private reasoning transcripts.

ReviewAssignment.title: review assignment payload
ReviewAssignment.description: assigns review responsibility to a human, model, evaluator, policy gate, or worker and identifies the artifact or decision under review.

ReviewEvent.title: review event payload
ReviewEvent.description: observable control-loop event that requests or performs review of an output, state, plan, safety condition, evidence bundle, or completion claim.

RevisionPlan.title: revision plan payload
RevisionPlan.description: planned remediation path that states what to change, which prior attempt it revises, and which criterion or feedback triggered the revision.

StopRetryLineage.title: stop retry lineage payload
StopRetryLineage.description: links a review, failure, or gate outcome to the bounded retry, revision, stop, or rollback path chosen for an orchestration loop.

ThinkAsTool.title: think as tool payload
ThinkAsTool.description: visible deliberation or review operation exposed as a bounded tool-like step with explicit inputs, outputs, and audit metadata.

ImprovementLoop.title: improvement loop payload
ImprovementLoop.description: An ImprovementLoop is a bounded control activity that receives an evaluated target and review or feedback evidence, forms revision plans, and produces one or more new attempts until success, failure, budget, safety, or human stop conditions hold.

loop_id.title: improvement-loop ID
loop_id.description: Stable identifier for one bounded review, revision, and retry loop.

maximum_attempts.title: maximum attempts
maximum_attempts.description: Maximum attempts the loop may contain before forced stopping.

status.title: loop status
status.description: Whether the loop is active, succeeded, failed, budget-stopped, or human-stopped.

evaluated_subject_id.title: evaluated-subject ID
evaluated_subject_id.description: Pins the subject version being improved by the loop.

stop_reason.title: stop reason
stop_reason.description: Success, failure, budget, safety, or human reason recorded when the loop terminates.

ReviewCoordination.title: review coordination payload
ReviewCoordination.description: ReviewCoordination organizes review responsibility, initiates review events, collects critique or feedback, and routes applicable results to tasks, roles, revision plans, gates, or improvement loops.

RevisionArtifact.title: revision artifact payload
RevisionArtifact.description: A RevisionArtifact is information produced or used by review or optimization to state findings, improvement grounds, proposed changes, or stop/retry lineage and traceable to the evaluated target and source evidence.

BranchCondition.title: branch condition payload
BranchCondition.description: condition over task state, trace evidence, policy, feedback, or capability match that determines which branch may run next.

DownstreamOperation.title: downstream operation payload
DownstreamOperation.description: DownstreamOperation is reference information for an executable branch, tool, protocol, or stop path; it is not a Route or Gate subtype.

Gate.title: gate payload
Gate.description: Gate is a ControlGate capability that allows, blocks, redirects, or escalates work based on conditions.

GateCondition.title: gate condition payload
GateCondition.description: condition checked at a workflow gate to decide whether control may proceed, block, redirect, escalate, retry, or stop.

GateOutcome.title: gate outcome payload
GateOutcome.description: GateOutcome is the observable allowed, blocked, redirected, or escalated state produced by evaluation, not a kind of gate.

RetryPolicy.title: retry policy payload
RetryPolicy.description: policy bounding when an orchestration loop may retry work, including retry count, changed context, backoff, escalation, and failure conditions.

Route.title: route payload
Route.description: Route is a RoutingSpecification declaring control flow to a typed target; the actual selection occurs in RoutingDecision.

RoutingDecision.title: routing decision payload
RoutingDecision.description: observable decision event that applies routing policy to select a routing target and record why that target was chosen.

decision_id.title: routing-decision ID
decision_id.description: Stable identifier for one replayable routing choice.

decided_at.title: decided at
decided_at.description: UTC instant at which the routing choice took effect.

reason.title: selection reason
reason.description: Explains how evaluated conditions and policy support the selected target.

RoutingPolicy.title: routing policy payload
RoutingPolicy.description: policy used by orchestration to choose a branch, worker, tool, protocol, retry, escalation, or stop path from current state and evidence.

RoutingTarget.title: routing target payload
RoutingTarget.description: typed endpoint selected by a route, such as a worker, subagent, tool, branch, protocol adapter, operation handle, or stop path.

StopCondition.title: stop condition payload
StopCondition.description: condition under which an orchestration loop must stop because success, failure, budget exhaustion, safety, or human decision has been reached.

RoutingSpecification.title: routing specification payload
RoutingSpecification.description: A RoutingSpecification is normative information describing eligible orchestration paths, selection policy, applicable conditions, retry bounds, or termination conditions that a routing decision can apply.

RoutingCondition.title: routing condition payload
RoutingCondition.description: A RoutingCondition is a RoutingSpecification evaluable from task state, trace evidence, policy, budget, safety, or human decision to enable a branch, pass a gate, or terminate flow.

ControlGate.title: control gate payload
ControlGate.description: A ControlGate is a capability at an orchestration control point that evaluates conditions and evidence, produces an auditable outcome, and allows, blocks, redirects, retries, escalates, or stops downstream work.

Goal.title: goal payload
Goal.description: high-level user or system intent that motivates an agent workflow before it is decomposed into operational objectives, tasks, or success criteria.

Objective.title: objective payload
Objective.description: operational and measurable target derived from a goal that can be assigned, checked, and traced through tasks, constraints, and completion criteria.

Task.title: task payload
Task.description: The legacy definition mixed task definition and execution instance; Task is narrowed to an assignable work specification, while a concrete execution is represented by RunAttempt.

task_id.title: task ID
task_id.description: Stable identifier for the task across its planning and execution lineage.

status.title: task status
status.description: Current state of the task in its controlled workflow lifecycle.

priority.title: priority
priority.description: Explicit scheduling priority; the project policy defines its numeric ordering.

constraint_summary.title: constraint summary
constraint_summary.description: Human-readable summary of the principal constraints governing task execution.

TaskCompletionCriterion.title: task completion criterion payload
TaskCompletionCriterion.description: states the observable condition used to decide whether a task, step, or work item has been completed satisfactorily.

TaskConstraint.title: task constraint payload
TaskConstraint.description: records a planning constraint such as deadline, budget, authority, capability, context, safety, or dependency limit that shapes task execution.

TaskDependency.title: task dependency payload
TaskDependency.description: states that one task, step, or work item depends on another result, condition, resource, or approval before it can proceed.

TaskPlan.title: task plan payload
TaskPlan.description: organizes a goal into ordered tasks, work items, dependencies, constraints, and completion criteria that can be delegated and audited.

plan_id.title: plan ID
plan_id.description: Stable identifier for the logical task plan across revisions.

version.title: plan version
version.description: Identifies one immutable revision of the task plan.

status.title: plan status
status.description: Controlled state such as drafted, reviewed, approved, executing, or closed.

created_at.title: created at
created_at.description: UTC instant at which this plan version was created.

TaskStep.title: task step payload
TaskStep.description: identifies one executable or reviewable step within a task plan, including ordering, dependency, owner, and completion evidence.

WorkItem.title: work item payload
WorkItem.description: assignable unit of planned work derived from a task and scoped to an actor, tool, subagent, route, or artifact outcome.

IntentionalEntity.title: intentional entity payload
IntentionalEntity.description: An IntentionalEntity is normative planning information expressing a desired future state, commitment to act, or measurable target that can be refined into work specifications.

Intent.title: intent payload
Intent.description: Intent is an IntentionalEntity adopted by a named actor that expresses commitment to act toward a goal within defined scope and constraints; it is not the goal, plan, or execution itself.

WorkSpecification.title: work specification payload
WorkSpecification.description: A WorkSpecification describes a unit or structure of work and specifies scope, performer requirements, inputs, expected outputs, and completion evidence, separately from actual execution activity.

TaskCondition.title: task condition payload
TaskCondition.description: A TaskCondition is an evaluable condition specification used by planning or control logic to govern whether a task may start, how it executes, or when it is complete, including criteria, constraints, and dependencies.

ActorAuthorityScope.title: actor authority scope payload
ActorAuthorityScope.description: authority-scope record that bounds the operations, resources, tools, data zones, and trust boundaries a runtime actor may access.

ActorCapabilityBinding.title: actor capability binding payload
ActorCapabilityBinding.description: binding record that connects a runtime actor to callable, observable, retrieval, generation, or review capabilities together with allowed conditions of use.

ActorRoleBinding.title: actor role binding payload
ActorRoleBinding.description: assignment record that binds a runtime actor to a responsibility-bearing role so delegation, review, approval, and accountability remain traceable.

AgentActor.title: agent actor payload
AgentActor.description: runtime identity capable of acting, observing, invoking tools, receiving delegation, emitting trace events, and being accountable for session activity.

actor_id.title: actor ID
actor_id.description: Stable identifier for the subject that bears actions or responsibilities within the system boundary.

actor_kind.title: actor kind
actor_kind.description: Controlled category distinguishing human, software, or external-service actors.

display_name.title: display name
display_name.description: Human-readable name for audit displays that does not determine identity.

DeveloperActor.title: developer actor payload
DeveloperActor.description: runtime participant responsible for developer-level instructions, implementation constraints, environment setup, or review decisions.

actor_id.title: actor ID
actor_id.description: Stable identifier for the subject that bears actions or responsibilities within the system boundary.

actor_kind.title: actor kind
actor_kind.description: Controlled category distinguishing human, software, or external-service actors.

display_name.title: display name
display_name.description: Human-readable name for audit displays that does not determine identity.

EmbeddingModel.title: embedding model payload
EmbeddingModel.description: An embedding model is a Model distinguished by producing representations for retrieval or matching.

ExternalServiceActor.title: external service actor payload
ExternalServiceActor.description: runtime participant outside the controlled system that can receive requests, return results, or introduce trust-boundary and provenance obligations.

actor_id.title: actor ID
actor_id.description: Stable identifier for the subject that bears actions or responsibilities within the system boundary.

actor_kind.title: actor kind
actor_kind.description: Controlled category distinguishing human, software, or external-service actors.

display_name.title: display name
display_name.description: Human-readable name for audit displays that does not determine identity.

GenerativeModel.title: generative model payload
GenerativeModel.description: A generative model is a Model distinguished by generating text, code, structured output, or call arguments.

HumanOperator.title: human operator payload
HumanOperator.description: person who can approve, pause, resume, redirect, review, or supply missing information during a runtime session.

actor_id.title: actor ID
actor_id.description: Stable identifier for the subject that bears actions or responsibilities within the system boundary.

actor_kind.title: actor kind
actor_kind.description: Controlled category distinguishing human, software, or external-service actors.

display_name.title: display name
display_name.description: Human-readable name for audit displays that does not determine identity.

ModelActor.title: model actor payload
ModelActor.description: model-backed runtime participant that generates, embeds, ranks, evaluates, transforms, or classifies information while producing traceable spans and budget use.

actor_id.title: actor ID
actor_id.description: Stable identifier for the subject that bears actions or responsibilities within the system boundary.

actor_kind.title: actor kind
actor_kind.description: Controlled category distinguishing human, software, or external-service actors.

display_name.title: display name
display_name.description: Human-readable name for audit displays that does not determine identity.

RemoteAgentReference.title: remote agent reference payload
RemoteAgentReference.description: It is reference information pointing to a remote actor; a reference is not an AgentActor and does not assert ownership of remote internals.

RerankerModel.title: reranker model payload
RerankerModel.description: A reranker model is a Model distinguished by reordering candidate items using relevance evidence.

ReviewerActor.title: reviewer actor payload
ReviewerActor.description: runtime participant that inspects outputs, trace evidence, artifacts, or policy-sensitive decisions and records review findings or approval state.

actor_id.title: actor ID
actor_id.description: Stable identifier for the subject that bears actions or responsibilities within the system boundary.

actor_kind.title: actor kind
actor_kind.description: Controlled category distinguishing human, software, or external-service actors.

display_name.title: display name
display_name.description: Human-readable name for audit displays that does not determine identity.

SystemServiceActor.title: system service actor payload
SystemServiceActor.description: runtime participant representing an internal service such as scheduler, sandbox, storage, telemetry, or policy mediator used by the agent system.

actor_id.title: actor ID
actor_id.description: Stable identifier for the subject that bears actions or responsibilities within the system boundary.

actor_kind.title: actor kind
actor_kind.description: Controlled category distinguishing human, software, or external-service actors.

display_name.title: display name
display_name.description: Human-readable name for audit displays that does not determine identity.

ToolServiceActor.title: tool service actor payload
ToolServiceActor.description: runtime participant representing a callable service or tool endpoint that executes requests and returns observable results, warnings, errors, or artifacts.

actor_id.title: actor ID
actor_id.description: Stable identifier for the subject that bears actions or responsibilities within the system boundary.

actor_kind.description: Controlled category distinguishing human, software, or external-service actors.

AgentTranscript.title: agent transcript payload
AgentTranscript.description: ordered runtime transcript of messages, actions, tool calls, model outputs, policy decisions, warnings, and artifact references that were actually visible or emitted.

AuditRecord.title: audit record payload
AuditRecord.description: raw audit evidence attached to a runtime session or trace, preserving what happened and who or what was associated without performing feedback interpretation.

Checkpoint.title: checkpoint payload
Checkpoint.description: saved continuation point that captures session state so a runtime can resume, branch, recover, inspect, or time travel from a known state.

CheckpointRestoreEvent.title: checkpoint restore event payload
CheckpointRestoreEvent.description: observable event that restores a runtime session, actor, graph, or workflow from a saved checkpoint and records restore provenance.

CommandExecution.title: command execution payload
CommandExecution.description: CommandExecution is a runtime activity observed by trace records; an activity is not a subtype of a record, span, or recorded event.

ObservableSummary.title: observable summary payload
ObservableSummary.description: observable runtime summary that compresses prior events, state, transcript, or artifact evidence for context budget, review, or handoff without storing private reasoning transcripts.

ReplayEvent.title: replay event payload
ReplayEvent.description: observable event that replays a trace, span, session segment, checkpoint, or artifact lineage for debugging, evaluation, reproduction, or review.

SpanAttribute.title: span attribute payload
SpanAttribute.description: keyed attribute attached to a trace span to record operation name, model/tool identity, status detail, latency, token use, or other observable metadata.

SpanStatus.title: span status payload
SpanStatus.description: status record for a trace span, such as unset, ok, error, cancelled, blocked, retried, or degraded, with optional diagnostic context.

StateDiff.title: state diff payload
StateDiff.description: difference record from one state snapshot to another, showing changed values, channels, artifacts, or lifecycle positions between two captured states.

StateSnapshot.title: state snapshot payload
StateSnapshot.description: captured runtime state for a session, actor, graph, tool, or statechart step, used for inspection, recovery, replay, or lifecycle transition.

TraceContext.title: trace context payload
TraceContext.description: correlation context carrying trace identifiers, parent context, sampling state, propagation metadata, and boundary information for a trace record.

TraceEvent.title: trace event payload
TraceEvent.description: observable runtime occurrence such as a message, action, tool call, state update, warning, policy decision, artifact update, or checkpoint event.

timestamp.title: Timestamp
timestamp.description: captures timestamp as a date-time scalar on agent-system artifacts, resources, events, policies, or adapter records.

TraceLink.title: trace link payload
TraceLink.description: non-parent causal or contextual association between spans, events, traces, attempts, or artifacts when ordinary parent-child trace structure is insufficient.

TraceRecord.title: trace record payload
TraceRecord.description: trace container for one runtime workflow or session segment, grouping spans, trace context, links, ordered events, retention policy, and root-span identity.

trace_id.title: trace ID
trace_id.description: Stable identifier correlating one end-to-end runtime trace across spans.

sampled.title: sampled
sampled.description: Whether this trace was retained by the applicable sampling decision.

TraceRetentionPolicy.title: trace retention policy payload
TraceRetentionPolicy.description: policy governing trace storage duration, sampling, redaction, visibility, export destination, deletion, privacy class, and no-retention modes.

TraceSpan.title: trace span payload
TraceSpan.description: operation interval or unit of work inside a trace, with parent span, timing, status, attributes, contained events, and links to related spans.

ObservabilityRecord.title: observability record payload
ObservabilityRecord.description: An ObservabilityRecord is time- and provenance-bearing information that preserves runtime behavior, state, messages, or accountability evidence with correlation identifiers, excluding unexternalized private reasoning.

StateRecord.title: state record payload
StateRecord.description: A StateRecord is an ObservabilityRecord whose primary content is runtime state at a version or time, a difference between versions, or a recoverable consistency boundary.

TraceMetadata.title: trace metadata payload
TraceMetadata.description: TraceMetadata is information attached to a trace or span to propagate context, express non-parental links, or record attributes and status without representing the observed activity itself.

ObservabilityEvent.title: observability event payload
ObservabilityEvent.description: An ObservabilityEvent is an event at a determinate time that changes or reports runtime state and can be correlated to a trace, session, or attempt; the occurrence is distinct from the record carrying it.

AgentSystem.title: agent system payload
AgentSystem.description: runtime arrangement that coordinates actors, models, tools, memory, policies, traces, sessions, and artifacts to perform bounded agent work.

system_id.title: System ID
system_id.description: captures identifier as a string scalar on agent-system artifacts, resources, events, policies, or adapter records.

Container.title: container payload
Container.description: describes a runtime execution environment with filesystem state, process isolation, network settings, and session-local resources.

environment_variables.title: environment variable
environment_variables.description: A name-value binding is a repeatable structural value of RuntimeEnvironment without independent business identity; keeping it as a node would turn a schema field into a concept.

ProcessHandle.title: process handle payload
ProcessHandle.description: identifies the operating-system process reference used to monitor, stop, or correlate an execution attempt.

RunAttempt.title: run attempt payload
RunAttempt.description: A run attempt is a bounded activity with retry lineage; it belongs to a session but is not a subtype of RuntimeSession.

status.title: Execution status
status.description: Records the controlled lifecycle state of one Run Attempt.

duration_ms.title: Duration milliseconds
duration_ms.description: captures duration in milliseconds as a integer scalar on agent-system artifacts, resources, events, policies, or adapter records.

retry_count.title: Retry count
retry_count.description: captures retry count as a integer scalar on agent-system artifacts, resources, events, policies, or adapter records.

legacy_status_text.title: Legacy status text
legacy_status_text.description: captures status as a string scalar on agent-system artifacts, resources, events, policies, or adapter records.

RunOutcome.title: run outcome payload
RunOutcome.description: records the terminal result of a run attempt or session, including success, failure, cancellation, produced artifacts, and final observable state.

RuntimeBudget.title: runtime budget payload
RuntimeBudget.description: runtime limit record for time, token, cost, retry, tool-call, context-window, or resource consumption constraints applied to an execution attempt or session.

cost.title: Cost
cost.description: captures cost as a number scalar on agent-system artifacts, resources, events, policies, or adapter records.

RuntimeEnvironment.title: runtime environment payload
RuntimeEnvironment.description: describes the concrete execution environment for a session, including container, sandbox, working directory, process settings, model/tool availability, and runtime configuration.

environment_variables.title: environment variable
environment_variables.description: A name-value binding is a repeatable structural value of RuntimeEnvironment without independent business identity; keeping it as a node would turn a schema field into a concept.

RuntimeSession.title: runtime session payload
RuntimeSession.description: A runtime session is a bounded execution episode grouping attempts, budget, environment, and artifacts under one identity; those items enter through composition relations.

lifecycle_state.title: lifecycle state
lifecycle_state.description: Current state in the controlled session lifecycle, not a separate graph node.

end_time.title: end time
end_time.description: UTC instant at which the session-end event took effect; null while active.

start_time.title: start time
start_time.description: UTC instant at which the session-start event took effect.

session_id.title: session ID
session_id.description: Stable identifier for this runtime session within one system deployment.

SessionEndEvent.title: session end event payload
SessionEndEvent.description: records the observable closure of a runtime session, including final status, cleanup, artifacts, and remaining audit references.

SessionPauseEvent.title: session pause event payload
SessionPauseEvent.description: records an observable transition where a session stops progressing while preserving resumable state and pending work.

SessionResumeEvent.title: session resume event payload
SessionResumeEvent.description: records the observable transition that restores a paused session from retained state, checkpoint, or external continuation.

SessionStartEvent.title: session start event payload
SessionStartEvent.description: records the observable event that opens a runtime session and binds the session to initial actor, environment, goal, and context state.

WorkingDirectory.title: working directory payload
WorkingDirectory.description: records the filesystem location that scopes relative paths and process execution for a runtime or tool invocation.

SessionLifecycleEvent.title: session lifecycle event payload
SessionLifecycleEvent.description: A SessionLifecycleEvent is an event at a determinate time that enters, exits, or restores a runtime-session lifecycle state while recording prior state, resulting state, actor, cause, and trace references.

FrameworkMapping.title: framework mapping payload
FrameworkMapping.description: A FrameworkMapping is a MappingRule for a stated framework and version that maps its agent, task, tool, handoff, state, memory, guardrail, result, or trace constructs to canonical concepts. Adapter.title: adapter payload
Adapter.description: An Adapter is an identity-bearing interoperability capability with version, applicable external system, direction, supported scope, and conformance statement. It applies MappingRule records to convert between external constructs and canonical concepts, relations, or embedded structure, explicitly reporting exact, lossy, unsupported, and round-trip outcomes. It does not copy the external class tree or alter canonical node identity.

MappingArtifact.title: mapping artifact payload
MappingArtifact.description: A MappingArtifact is an information object with identifier, version, author or producer, applicable mapping, and evidence provenance. It includes directional mapping rules, conversion warnings, mapping-test results, and coverage reports. These artifacts may appear in Adapter node details while retaining their own identity and provenance and do not form a second graph.

MappingTestResult.title: mapping test result payload
MappingTestResult.description: A MappingTestResult is a MappingArtifact recording the tested rule, source and target versions, test input, expected and actual output, exact/lossy/unsupported classification, round-trip comparison, pass status, execution time, and evidence. It verifies rather than replaces the rule.

CoverageReport.title: mapping coverage report payload
CoverageReport.description: A CoverageReport is a MappingArtifact for a specified Adapter, external version, and canonical version. It aggregates construct totals, mapped totals, exact/lossy/unsupported/untested counts, test-result references, known gaps, and release gates. It is an aggregate report and does not replace individual rules or test results.

CapabilityCandidate.title: capability candidate payload
CapabilityCandidate.description: capability candidate representing a possible tool, resource, prompt, API operation, or hosted capability under consideration during discovery and selection.

CapabilitySelectionDecision.title: capability selection decision payload
CapabilitySelectionDecision.description: selection event that chooses, rejects, or defers a capability candidate across tools, resources, prompts, and API operations.

selection_id.title: selection-decision ID
selection_id.description: Stable identifier for one candidate assessment and selection decision.

decided_at.title: decided at
decided_at.description: UTC instant at which the selection decision took effect.

reason.title: selection reason
reason.description: Explains the score, permission, and policy basis for selecting and rejecting candidates.

PromptCandidate.title: prompt candidate payload
PromptCandidate.description: prompt candidate returned by discovery, filtering, or protocol listing before a prompt selection decision chooses whether it may be retrieved or instantiated.

PromptSelectionDecision.title: prompt selection decision payload
PromptSelectionDecision.description: selection event that chooses, rejects, or defers a prompt candidate based on task fit, argument availability, authority, and workflow intent.

selection_id.title: selection-decision ID
selection_id.description: Stable identifier for one candidate assessment and selection decision.

decided_at.title: decided at
decided_at.description: UTC instant at which the selection decision took effect.

reason.title: selection reason
reason.description: Explains the score, permission, and policy basis for selecting and rejecting candidates.

ResourceCandidate.title: resource candidate payload
ResourceCandidate.description: resource candidate returned by discovery, filtering, or protocol listing before a resource selection decision chooses whether it may be read, subscribed, or staged.

ResourceSelectionDecision.title: resource selection decision payload
ResourceSelectionDecision.description: selection event that chooses, rejects, or defers a resource candidate based on relevance, authorization, freshness, data zone, and context need.

selection_id.title: selection-decision ID
selection_id.description: Stable identifier for one candidate assessment and selection decision.

decided_at.title: decided at
decided_at.description: UTC instant at which the selection decision took effect.

reason.title: selection reason
reason.description: Explains the score, permission, and policy basis for selecting and rejecting candidates.

ToolCandidate.title: Tool Candidate payload
ToolCandidate.description: A ToolCandidate is a Candidate whose considered object is a Tool and whose matching evidence is represented by ToolMatch-assesses-ToolCandidate.

ToolCandidateSet.title: Tool Candidate Set payload
ToolCandidateSet.description: A ToolCandidateSet is a DiscoveryCandidateSet whose members are ToolCandidate records connected through ToolCandidateSet-contains-ToolCandidate.

ToolDisambiguationPrompt.title: Tool Disambiguation Prompt payload
ToolDisambiguationPrompt.description: A disambiguation prompt is an information artifact requesting clarification, not a candidate, match, or selection-decision subtype.

ToolEmbeddingMatch.title: Tool Embedding Match payload
ToolEmbeddingMatch.description: A ToolEmbeddingMatch is a ToolMatch whose evidence is vector similarity between a search query and a tool representation.

ToolFallback.title: Tool Fallback payload
ToolFallback.description: Fallback specifies an alternative rule after selection failure and can exist before a decision, so it is not a SelectionDecision subtype.

ToolMatch.title: Tool Match Assessment payload
ToolMatch.description: A ToolMatch is a MatchAssessment that evaluates a ToolCandidate against a Tool using name, signature, embedding, regular-expression, or task-fit evidence.

ToolRegexMatch.title: Tool Regex Match payload
ToolRegexMatch.description: A ToolRegexMatch is a ToolMatch whose evidence is a regular-expression match over a query and tool metadata.

ToolSearch.title: Tool Search payload
ToolSearch.description: A ToolSearch is a DiscoveryActivity that consumes a ToolSearchQuery and produces a ToolCandidateSet by retrieving ToolDefinition records.

ToolSearchQuery.title: Tool Search Query payload
ToolSearchQuery.description: A query is an information expression consumed by search, not a subtype of the search activity or a candidate.

ToolSelectionDecision.title: Tool Selection Decision payload
ToolSelectionDecision.description: A ToolSelectionDecision is a SelectionDecision that considers ToolMatch evidence and either selects a Tool or rejects a ToolCandidate.

selection_id.title: selection-decision ID
selection_id.description: Stable identifier for one candidate assessment and selection decision.

decided_at.title: decided at
decided_at.description: UTC instant at which the selection decision took effect.

reason.title: selection reason
reason.description: Explains the score, permission, and policy basis for selecting and rejecting candidates.

DiscoveryActivity.title: Discovery Activity payload
DiscoveryActivity.description: DiscoveryActivity consumes a query or requirement, searches one or more discoverable surfaces, and produces a candidate set; it does not itself make the final selection decision.

Candidate.title: Candidate Record payload
Candidate.description: Candidate records an object considered under a particular query and discovery activity, including object reference, source, and preliminary applicability; candidacy does not imply selection or authorization.

DiscoveryCandidateSet.title: Discovery Candidate Set payload
DiscoveryCandidateSet.description: DiscoveryCandidateSet aggregates candidate records from the same query and discovery batch and records completeness, pre-ranking state, and provenance; it does not denote memory-retrieval candidate sets, and rankings or selection decisions are not member types.

MatchAssessment.title: Match Assessment payload
MatchAssessment.description: MatchAssessment records a candidate's fit to a query, requirement, or constraint, including method, value, threshold, and provenance; it supports but is not the selection decision.

SelectionDecision.title: Selection Decision payload
SelectionDecision.description: SelectionDecision is a provenance-bearing decision over a candidate set, recording selected or rejected objects, rationale, authority/policy evidence, and decider; it follows discovery and assessment and is not a candidate.

selection_id.title: selection-decision ID
selection_id.description: Stable identifier for one candidate assessment and selection decision.

decided_at.title: decided at
decided_at.description: UTC instant at which the selection decision took effect.

reason.title: selection reason
reason.description: Explains the score, permission, and policy basis for selecting and rejecting candidates.

Command.title: Command payload
Command.description: describes a tool- or runtime-level operation request that may be invoked with arguments, permissions, working directory, and observable results.

execution_area.title: Command Execution Area
execution_area.description: The execution area is a command working-directory/surface scope without independent identity and becomes a Command field.

arguments.title: Command Argument
arguments.description: An argument is a bound value of Command and is represented more accurately as a repeatable structure field.

ConditionalToolEnabled.title: conditional tool enabled payload
ConditionalToolEnabled.description: It is a condition specification governing when a tool is available, not a subtype of Tool, Call, or Attempt.

ExecutionRequest.title: Execution Request payload
ExecutionRequest.description: An ExecutionRequest is an InvocationSpecification that asks a runtime to run a command, call a service, access a resource, or execute a tool operation.

ExecutionResult.title: Execution Result payload
ExecutionResult.description: An ExecutionResult is an InvocationResult for an ExecutionRequest that carries observable output, error stream, exit status, or artifact references without asserting task success.

PreExecutionSafetyCheck.title: pre-execution safety check payload
PreExecutionSafetyCheck.description: The safety check is a pre-execution governance activity related to a plan, not a subtype of the plan or call.

ProgrammaticToolCall.title: Programmatic Tool Call payload
ProgrammaticToolCall.description: A ProgrammaticToolCall is a ToolCall initiated by generated code, runtime orchestration, or explicit API logic rather than direct natural-language selection.

arguments.title: arguments
arguments.description: Structured argument values validated against the referenced ToolDefinition.

call_id.title: tool-call ID
call_id.description: Stable identifier for one logical tool call shared by its retry attempts.

requested_at.title: requested at
requested_at.description: UTC instant at which the call was requested.

idempotency_key.title: idempotency key
idempotency_key.description: Caller-provided key preventing duplicate effects when supported by the tool.

caller_id.title: caller ID
caller_id.description: Identifies the actor requesting the tool call.

operation_id.title: operation ID
operation_id.description: Pins the tool operation to execute for this call.

PromptGetRequest.title: prompt get request payload
PromptGetRequest.description: event request to retrieve a prompt definition or template from a registry or protocol server before argument binding and context staging.

PromptInstantiation.title: prompt instantiation payload
PromptInstantiation.description: Prompt instantiation is the activity of binding arguments to a definition, not a subtype of template, call, or result.

ResourceReadRequest.title: resource read request payload
ResourceReadRequest.description: event request to read a named resource from a server or connector, including resource identity, access scope, authorization context, and expected representation.

ResourceReadResult.title: resource read result payload
ResourceReadResult.description: observable resource content and metadata returned by a resource read request, including representation, provenance, cache hints, warning state, and context-ingress eligibility.

ResourceSubscription.title: resource subscription payload
ResourceSubscription.description: A resource subscription is an identified ongoing specification of selectors, delivery, and cancellation, not a single invocation.

ShellCommand.title: Shell Command payload
ShellCommand.description: describes a shell-level command invocation with command text, arguments, environment, working directory, and sandbox or permission context.

execution_area.title: Command Execution Area
execution_area.description: The execution area is a command working-directory/surface scope without independent identity and becomes a Command field.

arguments.title: Command Argument
arguments.description: An argument is a bound value of Command and is represented more accurately as a repeatable structure field.

ToolApprovalGate.title: tool approval gate payload
ToolApprovalGate.description: A tool approval gate is a pre-execution control specification referencing safety authorization decisions, not a Call or Attempt subtype.

ToolCall.title: Tool Call payload
ToolCall.description: A ToolCall is an Invocation that identifies a caller and operation, supplies arguments, and requests a selected Tool; call_id, requested_at, operation_id, and idempotency_key make the request traceable and replay-safe.

arguments.title: arguments
arguments.description: Structured argument values validated against the referenced ToolDefinition.

call_id.title: tool-call ID
call_id.description: Stable identifier for one logical tool call shared by its retry attempts.

requested_at.title: requested at
requested_at.description: UTC instant at which the call was requested.

idempotency_key.title: idempotency key
idempotency_key.description: Caller-provided key preventing duplicate effects when supported by the tool.

caller_id.title: caller ID
caller_id.description: Identifies the actor requesting the tool call.

operation_id.title: operation ID
operation_id.description: Pins the tool operation to execute for this call.

ToolCallAttempt.title: Tool Call Attempt payload
ToolCallAttempt.description: A ToolCallAttempt is an InvocationAttempt that records one execution attempt of a ToolCall, identified by attempt_id and attempt_number, with status, timing, authorization, sandbox, and result context.
