attempt_id.title: attempt ID
attempt_id.description: Stable identifier for one actual execution attempt; every retry gets a new identifier.

attempt_number.title: attempt number
attempt_number.description: One-based attempt number within the same logical call.

started_at.title: started at
started_at.description: UTC instant at which this execution attempt started.

status.title: attempt status
status.description: Active, succeeded, failed, timed-out, or cancelled state of the attempt.

timeout_ms.title: timeout milliseconds
timeout_ms.description: Maximum milliseconds this attempt may run before timing out.

sandbox_id.title: sandbox ID
sandbox_id.description: Identifies the sandbox isolating process, filesystem, and network for this attempt.

authorization_id.title: authorization ID
authorization_id.description: Identifies the authorization grant or approval governing this attempt.

ToolCallPlan.title: tool call plan payload
ToolCallPlan.description: planned invocation artifact that proposes a candidate tool action, constructed arguments, approval requirements, expected result handling, retry policy, and pre-execution safety context.

ToolCallRetry.title: Tool Call Retry payload
ToolCallRetry.description: A ToolCallRetry is a ToolCallAttempt initiated to retry a prior ToolCallAttempt after a retryable failure or policy decision and linked by ToolCallRetry-retries-ToolCallAttempt.

attempt_id.title: attempt ID
attempt_id.description: Stable identifier for one actual execution attempt; every retry gets a new identifier.

attempt_number.title: attempt number
attempt_number.description: One-based attempt number within the same logical call.

started_at.title: started at
started_at.description: UTC instant at which this execution attempt started.

status.title: attempt status
status.description: Active, succeeded, failed, timed-out, or cancelled state of the attempt.

timeout_ms.title: timeout milliseconds
timeout_ms.description: Maximum milliseconds this attempt may run before timing out.

sandbox_id.title: sandbox ID
sandbox_id.description: Identifies the sandbox isolating process, filesystem, and network for this attempt.

authorization_id.title: authorization ID
authorization_id.description: Identifies the authorization grant or approval governing this attempt.

ToolDescriptionTrust.title: tool description trust payload
ToolDescriptionTrust.description: Description trust is a governed quality attached to a tool description or source, not a definition or invocation stage.

ToolError.title: tool error payload
ToolError.description: diagnostic resource describing an error, exception, timeout, validation failure, or execution failure associated with a tool call attempt.

ToolResult.title: Tool Result payload
ToolResult.description: A ToolResult is an InvocationResult produced by a ToolCallAttempt, identified by result_id, status, received_at, and payload_digest, and carrying output, error, warning, or metadata without asserting task success.

result_id.title: result ID
result_id.description: Stable identifier for the tool result returned by one call attempt.

status.title: result status
status.description: Distinguishes success, partial success, or protocol-level failure results.

received_at.title: received at
received_at.description: UTC instant at which the runtime received the result.

payload_digest.title: payload digest
payload_digest.description: Immutable content digest of the raw result payload for integrity and deduplication.

ToolResultObservation.title: Tool Result Observation payload
ToolResultObservation.description: The observation records how a result is interpreted, traced, or staged into context and has provenance distinct from the raw result.

ToolSideEffect.title: tool side effect payload
ToolSideEffect.description: tool-local record of an external state change or persistent effect produced or attempted by a tool execution before it is connected to commit approval, rollback, audit, and canonical safety SideEffect evidence.

ToolWarning.title: tool warning payload
ToolWarning.description: non-fatal warning or diagnostic resource attached to a tool result, argument validation, degraded execution, fallback, unsafe content signal, or partial response.

UnsafeArgumentPattern.title: unsafe argument pattern payload
UnsafeArgumentPattern.description: An unsafe-argument pattern is a versionable detection/governance specification related to arguments, not an Argument or Call subtype.

InvocationSpecification.title: Invocation Specification payload
InvocationSpecification.description: InvocationSpecification is pre-call static or planning information specifying operation, arguments, approval, timeout, idempotency, and result handling; it does not assert that a call occurred.

Invocation.title: Invocation payload
Invocation.description: Invocation represents an initiated call activity with caller, target operation, and bound arguments; it is constrained by a plan and can have one or more Attempts, but is not an Attempt, Result, or Outcome.

InvocationAttempt.title: Invocation Attempt payload
InvocationAttempt.description: InvocationAttempt is one execution attempt for an Invocation with attempt identity, start/end time, status, timeout, and retry lineage; every retry creates a new Attempt.

InvocationResult.title: Invocation Result payload
InvocationResult.description: InvocationResult is the direct return record of an Attempt, storing content, status, metadata, and provenance; diagnostics may accompany it, side effects may be caused by the same Attempt, and business Outcome is interpreted at a higher level.

InvocationEffect.title: Invocation Effect payload
InvocationEffect.description: InvocationEffect records an external, persistent, or compensable change produced or attempted by an invocation attempt; it can coexist with a return result and connects by governance relations to approval, audit, and recovery.

InvocationDiagnostic.title: Invocation Diagnostic payload
InvocationDiagnostic.description: InvocationDiagnostic is call-scoped information explaining error, warning, timeout, or partial execution; a non-fatal diagnostic can coexist with InvocationResult, and the occurrence can map to a Feedback FailureEvent.

AdditionalInput.title: additional input payload
AdditionalInput.description: Additional input is data requested by elicitation, not a subtype of interaction, participant, or capability.

MCPAuthorization.title: MCP authorization payload
MCPAuthorization.description: MCP authorization is consent, grant, or scope evidence carried by a session, not a participant or transport.

MCPClient.title: MCP client payload
MCPClient.description: protocol participant object that opens MCP sessions, discovers server capabilities, requests prompts or resources, invokes tools, and carries authorization context for an agent runtime.

MCPElicitation.title: MCP Elicitation payload
MCPElicitation.description: MCPElicitation is an MCPInteraction in which an MCP server requests AdditionalInput from a client or user during an active interaction, subject to client consent and policy.

MCPPromptList.title: MCP prompt list payload
MCPPromptList.description: server-exposed list of prompt definitions and prompt templates available for retrieval, argument binding, workflow staging, or guided interaction.

MCPResourceList.title: MCP resource list payload
MCPResourceList.description: server-exposed list of resource definitions and resource templates available for read, subscribe, discovery, or context/data access operations.

MCPResourceTemplateList.title: MCP resource template list payload
MCPResourceTemplateList.description: server-exposed list of resource templates whose parameters can be bound to discover or read families of MCP resources.

MCPRootList.title: MCP root list payload
MCPRootList.description: client-provided list of roots or workspace scopes that bound which files, directories, repositories, or resource regions an MCP server may access.

MCPSamplingRequest.title: MCP sampling request payload
MCPSamplingRequest.description: protocol request in which an MCP server asks the client or host to perform model sampling under the client's policy, context, and authorization controls.

MCPServer.title: MCP server payload
MCPServer.description: protocol participant object that exposes tools, resources, prompts, templates, and capability metadata to MCP clients over a session and transport.

MCPServerCapability.title: MCP server capability payload
MCPServerCapability.description: capability metadata advertised by an MCP server, including supported tool, resource, prompt, sampling, roots, elicitation, authorization, and transport features.

MCPSession.title: MCP Session payload
MCPSession.description: records the MCP client/server connection context in which capabilities, requests, responses, authorization, and transport metadata are exchanged.

mcp_session_id.title: MCP session ID
mcp_session_id.description: Stable identifier for one MCP session between a client and server.

protocol_version.title: protocol version
protocol_version.description: MCP protocol version negotiated during initialization and used by this session.

state.title: session state
state.description: State in the initialized, active, closed, or failed MCP lifecycle.

MCPToolList.title: MCP tool list payload
MCPToolList.description: server-exposed list of tool definitions and callable capability descriptors available for discovery, filtering, approval, and invocation by an MCP client.

MCPTransport.title: MCP transport payload
MCPTransport.description: protocol channel and delivery surface used by MCP client/server sessions to exchange JSON-RPC messages, capability metadata, tool calls, resource reads, prompt requests, and elicitation traffic.

transport_kind.title: Transport kind
transport_kind.description: Identifies the negotiated transport mechanism used by an MCP session.

MCPParticipant.title: MCP Participant payload
MCPParticipant.description: MCPParticipant is a role joining an MCP session with endpoint identity and sending or receiving protocol messages; Client and Server specialize it by responsibility and message direction.

MCPCapability.title: MCP Capability payload
MCPCapability.description: MCPCapability represents a protocol function declared through initialization or negotiation, such as tools, resources, prompts, sampling, roots, or elicitation support; capability declarations are distinct from lists containing definitions.

MCPDefinitionList.title: MCP Definition List payload
MCPDefinitionList.description: MCPDefinitionList is a collection with list version, pagination, or change-notification semantics whose members are tool, resource, template, prompt, or root entries; a capability enables publication, but the list is not itself a capability.

MCPInteraction.title: MCP Interaction payload
MCPInteraction.description: MCPInteraction records protocol request/response correlation, direction, participants, time, and result state within a session; sampling and elicitation specialize by purpose, while input content and authorization evidence connect by relations.

APIOperation.title: API operation payload
APIOperation.description: external API operation action with method, endpoint, arguments schema, result schema, authentication requirements, side-effect expectations, and observable invocation result.

CapabilityDescriptor.title: capability descriptor payload
CapabilityDescriptor.description: machine-readable capability descriptor that records operation identity, schema surface, trust metadata, permission requirements, discovery labels, and protocol exposure for a tool, resource, prompt, or API operation.

CapabilitySurface.title: capability surface payload
CapabilitySurface.description: A capability surface is a collection publishing definitions and descriptors, not a subtype of Tool or Capability.

ComputerTool.title: computer tool payload
ComputerTool.description: object type for a computer, browser, desktop UI, or screen-control tool that acts on an interactive environment and produces observable UI or environment state.

FunctionTool.title: function tool payload
FunctionTool.description: object type for a function tool whose invocation is described by a callable name, schema for arguments, return schema, and host-side implementation.

HostedMCPTool.title: hosted MCP tool payload
HostedMCPTool.description: object type for an MCP-hosted tool exposed by a server to a client along with protocol metadata, authorization context, schema surface, and invocation constraints.

HostedTool.title: hosted tool payload
HostedTool.description: object type for a hosted tool backed by an external service or provider-managed runtime whose execution surface is invoked through a registry or protocol connector.

LocalRuntimeTool.title: local runtime tool payload
LocalRuntimeTool.description: object type for a local runtime tool that executes through a process, shell, computer-control, code, browser, or container surface controlled by the host.

PromptDefinition.title: prompt definition payload
PromptDefinition.description: prompt definition resource that describes a reusable prompt template and workflow pattern, expected arguments, output role, and applicability conditions exposed by a server or registry.

ResourceDefinition.title: resource definition payload
ResourceDefinition.description: resource definition that describes how a protocol or server exposes context/data for list, read, subscribe, and resource-template operations.

ShellTool.title: shell tool payload
ShellTool.description: object type for a shell or command runtime tool that executes command lines, scripts, or process operations under sandbox and policy constraints.

Tool.title: Tool payload
Tool.description: A Tool is a callable capability that exposes an APIOperation, accepts arguments under its definition and permission constraints, and yields an observable result when invoked.

ToolCapability.title: Tool Capability payload
ToolCapability.description: advertised operation or capability surface of a tool, including its execution boundary, permission requirements, schema surface, and observable result type.

ToolDefinition.title: tool definition payload
ToolDefinition.description: schema-like resource describing a tool's name, purpose, callable operation, argument schema, result schema, permission declaration, constraints, version, and deprecation state.

argument_schema.title: argument structure
argument_schema.description: Argument structure embedded as node information; it does not create a Schema graph node.

description.title: Tool Description
description.description: The legacy item is only definition text without separate identity or provenance, so it becomes ToolDefinition.description.

name.title: tool name
name.description: Stable name by which callers refer to the tool.

result_schema.title: result structure
result_schema.description: Result structure embedded as node information; it does not create a Schema graph node.

version.title: definition version
version.description: Identifies one immutable version of the argument, result, and permission contract.

definition_id.title: definition ID
definition_id.description: Stable identifier for the logical tool definition across versions.

ToolDeprecationNotice.title: Tool Deprecation Notice payload
ToolDeprecationNotice.description: A ToolDeprecationNotice is ToolMetadata that identifies a deprecated ToolDefinition and records lifecycle or replacement guidance rather than describing the callable capability itself.

ToolPermissionSpec.title: tool permission spec payload
ToolPermissionSpec.description: tool-side permission declaration that states required scopes, approval behavior, protected resources, safety constraints, and policy hooks without replacing a policy decision.

ToolRegistry.title: tool registry payload
ToolRegistry.description: catalog resource that stores available tool definitions, capability descriptors, schemas, permissions, versions, discovery metadata, and protocol exposure records.

PromptTemplate.title: prompt template payload
PromptTemplate.description: templated message or workflow pattern whose variables can be bound during prompt instantiation before being staged into a model call or agent step.

ResourceTemplate.title: resource template payload
ResourceTemplate.description: parameterized resource template that binds variables to resource URIs or selectors so related resources can be discovered and read safely.

ToolSpecification.title: Tool Registry Specification payload
ToolSpecification.description: ToolSpecification is a static registry specification with stable identity, version, and constraints; it can specify how a tool, prompt, or resource is discovered and used, but is not a runtime call, attempt, or result.

ToolMetadata.title: Tool Metadata Record payload
ToolMetadata.description: ToolMetadata is a separately referencable, versioned, or governed descriptive record such as a capability descriptor or deprecation notice; ordinary names, version strings, and description text remain ToolDefinition fields.

AuditDisclosure.title: audit disclosure payload
AuditDisclosure.description: records the auditable disclosure decision for released, redacted, or suppressed content, including filter, policy, recipient boundary, sensitive span, and trace evidence.

CommitApproval.title: commit approval payload
CommitApproval.description: records an approval outcome that authorizes a bounded side effect, including approver or policy authority, protected resource, scope, conditions, expiry, and trace evidence.

decision_kind.title: decision kind
decision_kind.description: Allow, deny, or escalate outcome produced by policy evaluation.

decision_id.title: policy-decision ID
decision_id.description: Stable identifier for one policy decision over subject, action, resource, and context.

reason.title: decision reason
reason.description: Explains how applicable rules, context, and evidence support the outcome.

decided_at.title: decided at
decided_at.description: UTC instant at which the policy decision took effect.

policy_version.title: policy version
policy_version.description: Pins the immutable policy version used for this decision.

subject_id.title: subject ID
subject_id.description: Identifies the subject whose authority is evaluated.

action.title: action
action.description: Canonical action name evaluated by this policy decision.

resource_id.title: resource ID
resource_id.description: Identifies the tool, data, or system resource on which the action operates.

context_digest.title: context digest
context_digest.description: Pins environment, task, and risk context used by the decision without copying sensitive content.

issuer_id.title: issuer ID
issuer_id.description: Identifies the policy-enforcement actor issuing and owning the decision.

valid_until.title: valid until
valid_until.description: Latest UTC instant until which enforcement may reuse this decision.

revoked_at.title: revoked at
revoked_at.description: UTC instant at which the decision was revoked early; null when not revoked.

CommitDenial.title: commit denial payload
CommitDenial.description: records a denial outcome that blocks a requested side effect, including policy basis, denial reason, affected resource, actor, and audit trace.

decision_kind.title: decision kind
decision_kind.description: Allow, deny, or escalate outcome produced by policy evaluation.

decision_id.title: policy-decision ID
decision_id.description: Stable identifier for one policy decision over subject, action, resource, and context.

reason.title: decision reason
reason.description: Explains how applicable rules, context, and evidence support the outcome.

decided_at.title: decided at
decided_at.description: UTC instant at which the policy decision took effect.

policy_version.title: policy version
policy_version.description: Pins the immutable policy version used for this decision.

subject_id.title: subject ID
subject_id.description: Identifies the subject whose authority is evaluated.

action.title: action
action.description: Canonical action name evaluated by this policy decision.

resource_id.title: resource ID
resource_id.description: Identifies the tool, data, or system resource on which the action operates.

context_digest.title: context digest
context_digest.description: Pins environment, task, and risk context used by the decision without copying sensitive content.

issuer_id.title: issuer ID
issuer_id.description: Identifies the policy-enforcement actor issuing and owning the decision.

valid_until.title: valid until
valid_until.description: Latest UTC instant until which enforcement may reuse this decision.

revoked_at.title: revoked at
revoked_at.description: UTC instant at which the decision was revoked early; null when not revoked.

CommitGate.title: side-effect commit gate payload
CommitGate.description: represents a side-effect gate that blocks or delays writes, external actions, network effects, commits, or disclosures until policy checks, approvals, or rollback controls are satisfied.

CommitRequest.title: commit request payload
CommitRequest.description: records a request to perform a side effect such as file write, repository commit, network mutation, external API action, memory write, or disclosure release.

DisclosureFilter.title: disclosure filter payload
DisclosureFilter.description: applies disclosure policy to output windows, context packages, artifacts, traces, or messages, deciding whether content is released, redacted, suppressed, or escalated.

DisclosureRule.title: disclosure rule payload
DisclosureRule.description: defines a safety or policy rule that determines when content may be revealed, redacted, delayed, summarized, or blocked for a recipient.

ProgressiveDisclosure.title: progressive disclosure payload
ProgressiveDisclosure.description: records a controlled release pattern in which content is revealed in stages under policy, trust-boundary, and context-window constraints.

Redaction.title: redaction activity payload
Redaction.description: records the operation that masks, removes, replaces, summarizes, or withholds sensitive content before it is shown, exported, stored, or sent across a boundary.

redaction_id.title: redaction ID
redaction_id.description: Stable identifier for one auditable information-redaction action.

applied_at.title: applied at
applied_at.description: UTC instant at which the redaction rule was applied.

replacement_marker.title: replacement marker
replacement_marker.description: Display marker replacing sensitive content without disclosing it.

RedactionRule.title: redaction rule payload
RedactionRule.description: defines a policy rule for detecting, masking, suppressing, summarizing, or replacing sensitive spans before content crosses an output or trust boundary.

SensitiveSpan.title: sensitive span payload
SensitiveSpan.description: identifies a span of output, source, message, trace, artifact, or memory content that requires redaction, suppression, masking, delayed disclosure, or restricted routing.

SideEffect.title: side effect payload
SideEffect.description: records an externally visible or persistent effect produced by a tool call, sandbox command, network call, memory write, file write, commit, or protocol operation.

SuppressedOutput.title: suppressed output payload
SuppressedOutput.description: records output withheld from a viewer or downstream context because of policy, privacy, safety, boundary, or budget constraints.

DisclosureControlActivity.title: disclosure-control activity payload
DisclosureControlActivity.description: A DisclosureControlActivity is performed by an identified actor for a stated recipient and trust boundary, applying disclosure rules to output, messages, artifacts, traces, or memory content. It records input content, sensitive spans, rule version, selection outcome, generated content, and audit evidence. Rules and result information are not activity subtypes.

DefenseFinding.title: defense finding payload
DefenseFinding.description: records a safety finding emitted by injection defense or taint analysis before it is converted into feedback metrics, review findings, blocking errors, or policy decisions.

risk_level.title: Risk level
risk_level.description: Represents the reviewed severity of a defense finding.

finding_id.title: defense-finding ID
finding_id.description: Stable identifier for one security finding formed by scanning or taint analysis.

confidence.title: confidence
confidence.description: Zero-to-one confidence assigned by the detection activity to the finding.

risk_score.title: risk score
risk_score.description: Zero-to-one risk score combining impact and likelihood.

observed_at.title: observed at
observed_at.description: UTC instant at which the detection activity established the finding.

IndirectInjectionSignal.title: indirect injection signal payload
IndirectInjectionSignal.description: records evidence that untrusted external content can influence agent behavior after retrieval, summarization, memory persistence, tool observation, or protocol translation.

InjectionScanResult.title: injection scan result payload
InjectionScanResult.description: records the result of scanning content for injection or taint risk, including matched signal, affected span, source, confidence, policy decision, and downstream sink if known.

InjectionSignature.title: injection signature specification payload
InjectionSignature.description: An InjectionSignature is a detection pattern representing indicators of prompt injection, tool-stream manipulation, or malicious instruction propagation and used as the target of scans_for_signature.

InstructionConflict.title: instruction conflict payload
InstructionConflict.description: records a safety-relevant conflict between trusted instructions and untrusted or lower-authority instruction candidates, including affected scope and resolution.

MaliciousToolOutput.title: malicious tool output payload
MaliciousToolOutput.description: records tool, resource, sandbox, or remote-agent output that carries untrusted instructions, exfiltration cues, policy-bypass attempts, deceptive tool guidance, or prompt-injection content.

MemoryPoisoningSignal.title: memory poisoning signal payload
MemoryPoisoningSignal.description: records evidence that persistent memory, retrieved chunks, indexes, summaries, or long-term context may contain instructions or data intended to corrupt later agent behavior.

PatternScan.title: pattern scan payload
PatternScan.description: A PatternScan is a DetectionActivity that examines messages, retrieved chunks, tool results, or other untrusted content for InjectionSignature indicators or policy violations and produces an InjectionScanResult.

PersistentContextRisk.title: persistent context risk payload
PersistentContextRisk.description: records a risk that unsafe, stale, adversarial, or over-authoritative content will persist across sessions, retrieval, summaries, checkpoints, or memory writes.

PoisonedRetrievedChunk.title: poisoned retrieved chunk payload
PoisonedRetrievedChunk.description: identifies a retrieved memory or document chunk that contains unsafe instructions, misleading metadata, tainted spans, or prompt-injection content.

PoisonedToolDescription.title: poisoned tool description payload
PoisonedToolDescription.description: identifies a tool description, MCP tool list entry, capability advertisement, or help text that attempts to manipulate selection, arguments, permissions, or trust.

PromptInjectionSignal.title: prompt injection signal payload
PromptInjectionSignal.description: records evidence that a message, source span, retrieved content, or tool result contains instructions attempting to override trusted instructions, manipulate tool arguments, exfiltrate data, or bypass policy.

QuarantineAction.title: quarantine action payload
QuarantineAction.description: records an action that isolates content, tool output, retrieved memory, or artifact from normal context and execution flow until review or policy resolution.

ResourceContentPoisoning.title: resource content poisoning payload
ResourceContentPoisoning.description: ResourceContentPoisoning denotes an event or occurrence record that introduces adversarial content into a resource, not the PoisonedContent itself; a relation should point to the affected content.

RiskSink.title: risk sink payload
RiskSink.description: classifies the protected destination that tainted content may influence, such as tool invocation, credentialed request, filesystem write, memory update, network call, or final disclosure.

RiskSource.title: risk source payload
RiskSource.description: classifies the origin of a safety risk, such as external message, retrieved document, tool output, remote agent artifact, memory item, network response, or user-supplied file.

SanitizationAction.title: sanitization action payload
SanitizationAction.description: records an action that removes, rewrites, masks, neutralizes, or narrows unsafe content before it can affect context, tool arguments, memory, or disclosure.

SourceSinkFlow.title: source sink flow payload
SourceSinkFlow.description: records a source-to-sink risk path from untrusted content to a protected sink such as tool arguments, network calls, memory writes, side effects, or disclosed output.

TaintedSource.title: tainted source payload
TaintedSource.description: identifies an untrusted or policy-sensitive source whose content must be tracked before it enters context, memory, a tool argument, a network request, or disclosed output.

TaintedSpan.title: tainted span payload
TaintedSpan.description: identifies the exact span of message, source, retrieved chunk, tool result, or output segment that carries tainted content for downstream source-sink analysis.

TaintPropagation.title: taint propagation payload
TaintPropagation.description: reifies the propagation of taint from one source span, context package, memory item, tool result, or artifact to another observable object.

ToolSchemaPoisoning.title: tool schema poisoning payload
ToolSchemaPoisoning.description: ToolSchemaPoisoning denotes an event record of adversarial changes to schema, defaults, or descriptions; affected poisoned content is linked by relation rather than treating the process as content.

ToolStreamInjectionSignal.title: tool stream injection signal payload
ToolStreamInjectionSignal.description: records evidence that streamed tool output, tool result messages, logs, or remote artifacts attempt to inject instructions into later context, tool arguments, routing, or disclosure decisions.

TrustedInstructionOverride.title: trusted instruction override payload
TrustedInstructionOverride.description: records that a higher-authority instruction, policy, or approval overrides an untrusted or lower-authority instruction candidate for a bounded scope.

UntrustedInstructionCandidate.title: untrusted instruction candidate payload
UntrustedInstructionCandidate.description: records an instruction-like text or structured directive from an untrusted source before it is accepted, ignored, quarantined, overridden, or escalated.

ThreatSignal.title: threat signal payload
ThreatSignal.description: A ThreatSignal is evidence information with detector source, inspected subject, evidence location, threat category, confidence, and time. It indicates that prompts, external content, tool streams, or memory may carry injection or poisoning risk. It supports findings and decisions but is not a confirmed threat, risk state, or mitigation action.

PoisonedContent.title: poisoned content payload
PoisonedContent.description: PoisonedContent is an information object with provenance, affected range, poisoning category, detection evidence, and disposition state. Its text or structured fields contain adversarial material intended to alter instruction priority, tool selection, arguments, disclosure, or persistent memory. Poisoning and detection events connect by relation and are not members of this class.

DetectionActivity.title: detection activity payload
DetectionActivity.description: A DetectionActivity is performed by an identified detector at a stated time over content, messages, tool output, schemas, memory, or propagation paths. It has an input subject, method, configuration version, and execution context and produces separate scan results or findings; those results are not activity subtypes.

TaintEntity.title: taint-bearing entity payload
TaintEntity.description: A TaintEntity is a referenceable object assigned a taint identifier by detection or policy. It has provenance, taint category, confidence, exact range, lifecycle, and clearance conditions. Whole sources and precise spans may specialize it, while propagation actions and source/sink roles do not.

MitigationAction.title: mitigation action payload
MitigationAction.description: A MitigationAction is a controlled disposition triggered by a policy decision or authorization against an identified subject and risk. It has actor, time, scope, before/after state, validation, and failure or rollback outcome. Sanitization and quarantine specialize it; trusted-instruction override remains a governance-decision record rather than being forced into the activity hierarchy.

RiskFlowRole.title: risk-flow role payload
RiskFlowRole.description: RiskFlowRole is a context-dependent role stating that a message, tool output, memory item, argument, credentialed operation, or side effect acts as risk source or protected destination within a SourceSinkFlow. The same entity may bear different roles in different flows without changing its ontological type.

AllowDecision.title: allow decision payload
AllowDecision.description: records a policy decision that permits a bounded operation to proceed under stated scope, conditions, trace evidence, and expiry.

decision_kind.title: decision kind
decision_kind.description: Allow, deny, or escalate outcome produced by policy evaluation.

decision_id.title: policy-decision ID
decision_id.description: Stable identifier for one policy decision over subject, action, resource, and context.

reason.title: decision reason
reason.description: Explains how applicable rules, context, and evidence support the outcome.

decided_at.title: decided at
decided_at.description: UTC instant at which the policy decision took effect.

policy_version.title: policy version
policy_version.description: Pins the immutable policy version used for this decision.

subject_id.title: subject ID
subject_id.description: Identifies the subject whose authority is evaluated.

action.title: action
action.description: Canonical action name evaluated by this policy decision.

resource_id.title: resource ID
resource_id.description: Identifies the tool, data, or system resource on which the action operates.

context_digest.title: context digest
context_digest.description: Pins environment, task, and risk context used by the decision without copying sensitive content.

issuer_id.title: issuer ID
issuer_id.description: Identifies the policy-enforcement actor issuing and owning the decision.

valid_until.title: valid until
valid_until.description: Latest UTC instant until which enforcement may reuse this decision.

revoked_at.title: revoked at
revoked_at.description: UTC instant at which the decision was revoked early; null when not revoked.

AuthorizationGrant.title: authorization grant payload
AuthorizationGrant.description: records an authorization result or credential-like grant issued after a policy decision, including grant scope, protected resource, subject actor, validity window, revocation state, and audit basis.

CapabilityGrant.title: capability grant payload
CapabilityGrant.description: records a grant for a specific tool, capability, resource surface, or operation so authorization can be checked at the point of invocation rather than inferred from actor identity alone.

DenyDecision.title: deny decision payload
DenyDecision.description: records a policy decision that prevents an operation, tool call, network request, disclosure, or side effect from proceeding and preserves the denial reason.

decision_kind.title: decision kind
decision_kind.description: Allow, deny, or escalate outcome produced by policy evaluation.

decision_id.title: policy-decision ID
decision_id.description: Stable identifier for one policy decision over subject, action, resource, and context.

reason.title: decision reason
reason.description: Explains how applicable rules, context, and evidence support the outcome.

decided_at.title: decided at
decided_at.description: UTC instant at which the policy decision took effect.

policy_version.title: policy version
policy_version.description: Pins the immutable policy version used for this decision.

subject_id.title: subject ID
subject_id.description: Identifies the subject whose authority is evaluated.

action.title: action
action.description: Canonical action name evaluated by this policy decision.

resource_id.title: resource ID
resource_id.description: Identifies the tool, data, or system resource on which the action operates.

context_digest.title: context digest
context_digest.description: Pins environment, task, and risk context used by the decision without copying sensitive content.

issuer_id.title: issuer ID
issuer_id.description: Identifies the policy-enforcement actor issuing and owning the decision.

valid_until.title: valid until
valid_until.description: Latest UTC instant until which enforcement may reuse this decision.

revoked_at.title: revoked at
revoked_at.description: UTC instant at which the decision was revoked early; null when not revoked.

EscalationDecision.title: escalation decision payload
EscalationDecision.description: records a policy decision that routes a request to a higher-authority reviewer, human approval, stricter sandbox, or additional verification path.

decision_kind.title: decision kind
decision_kind.description: Allow, deny, or escalate outcome produced by policy evaluation.

decision_id.title: policy-decision ID
decision_id.description: Stable identifier for one policy decision over subject, action, resource, and context.

reason.title: decision reason
reason.description: Explains how applicable rules, context, and evidence support the outcome.

decided_at.title: decided at
decided_at.description: UTC instant at which the policy decision took effect.

policy_version.title: policy version
policy_version.description: Pins the immutable policy version used for this decision.

subject_id.title: subject ID
subject_id.description: Identifies the subject whose authority is evaluated.

action.title: action
action.description: Canonical action name evaluated by this policy decision.

resource_id.title: resource ID
resource_id.description: Identifies the tool, data, or system resource on which the action operates.

context_digest.title: context digest
context_digest.description: Pins environment, task, and risk context used by the decision without copying sensitive content.

issuer_id.title: issuer ID
issuer_id.description: Identifies the policy-enforcement actor issuing and owning the decision.

valid_until.title: valid until
valid_until.description: Latest UTC instant until which enforcement may reuse this decision.

revoked_at.title: revoked at
revoked_at.description: UTC instant at which the decision was revoked early; null when not revoked.

HumanApproval.title: human approval record payload
HumanApproval.description: records explicit human authorization or rejection for an action, permission request, side effect, disclosure, or policy exception with actor and trace provenance.

PermissionPrompt.title: permission prompt payload
PermissionPrompt.description: records a request for authority before an action proceeds, including requesting actor, operation, protected resource, scope, decision options, and approval channel.

PermissionResponse.title: permission response payload
PermissionResponse.description: records the response to a permission prompt, including allow, deny, escalate, conditionally allow, expiry, user or policy authority, and traceable rationale.

PermissionScope.title: permission scope payload
PermissionScope.description: bounds a single permission request or response by naming the requested operation, protected resource, actor, data zone, trust boundary, duration, and allowed side effects.

PolicyAction.title: policy action specification payload
PolicyAction.description: describes the enforcement action chosen by a policy decision, such as allow, deny, redact, quarantine, require approval, route to sandbox, limit network egress, or escalate.

PolicyCondition.title: policy condition payload
PolicyCondition.description: states a safety policy predicate over subject actor, requested operation, protected resource, data zone, trust boundary, policy basis, runtime state, and enforcement mode.

PolicyDecision.title: policy decision payload
PolicyDecision.description: records the concrete allow, deny, escalate, redact, quarantine, sandbox, or approval-required outcome produced by evaluating a policy rule against a requested operation.

decision_kind.title: decision kind
decision_kind.description: Allow, deny, or escalate outcome produced by policy evaluation.

decision_id.title: policy-decision ID
decision_id.description: Stable identifier for one policy decision over subject, action, resource, and context.

reason.title: decision reason
reason.description: Explains how applicable rules, context, and evidence support the outcome.

decided_at.title: decided at
decided_at.description: UTC instant at which the policy decision took effect.

policy_version.title: policy version
policy_version.description: Pins the immutable policy version used for this decision.

subject_id.title: subject ID
subject_id.description: Identifies the subject whose authority is evaluated.

action.title: action
action.description: Canonical action name evaluated by this policy decision.

resource_id.title: resource ID
resource_id.description: Identifies the tool, data, or system resource on which the action operates.

context_digest.title: context digest
context_digest.description: Pins environment, task, and risk context used by the decision without copying sensitive content.

issuer_id.title: issuer ID
issuer_id.description: Identifies the policy-enforcement actor issuing and owning the decision.

valid_until.title: valid until
valid_until.description: Latest UTC instant until which enforcement may reuse this decision.

revoked_at.title: revoked at
revoked_at.description: UTC instant at which the decision was revoked early; null when not revoked.

PolicyException.title: policy exception payload
PolicyException.description: records a bounded exception to a policy rule with exception scope, authorized actor, protected resource, expiration, revocation condition, compensating control, and audit reason.

PolicyRule.title: policy rule payload
PolicyRule.description: defines a safety or governance rule that can evaluate actor authority, requested operation, protected resource, data zone, trust boundary, runtime context, and enforcement action.

PolicySpecification.title: policy specification payload
PolicySpecification.description: A PolicySpecification is an identity-bearing, versioned normative information object with an issuer and applicability scope. It uses conditions, rules, actions, and exceptions to prescribe permitted, required, or forbidden behavior by subjects over protected resources in a context; actual evaluations and decisions reference it by relation.

AuthorizationArtifact.title: authorization artifact payload
AuthorizationArtifact.description: An AuthorizationArtifact is an auditable and revocable information artifact issued by an identifiable authority. It records the grantee, permitted actions, protected resources, context, permission scope, issue and expiry times, obligations, and originating decision. It evidences authorization and is neither a policy rule nor the approval activity.

DeniedNetworkCall.title: denied network call payload
DeniedNetworkCall.description: records a network call blocked by policy or sandbox control, including requested destination, protected data zone, denial reason, policy decision, and trace event.

network_call_id.title: network-call ID
network_call_id.description: Stable identifier for one actual network-interaction attempt.

method.title: method
method.description: Request method or operation name used by the network protocol.

started_at.title: started at
started_at.description: UTC instant at which the network call started.

status.title: call status
status.description: Whether the call was allowed, denied, succeeded, failed, or timed out.

DomainSocket.title: domain socket payload
DomainSocket.description: records use of a local domain socket as a communication surface whose boundary, permission, credential, and sandbox implications must be explicit.

FilesystemPolicy.title: filesystem policy payload
FilesystemPolicy.description: defines filesystem read, write, execute, path, retention, and redaction constraints applied to sandbox commands, tool calls, side effects, and artifacts.

HTTPProxy.title: HTTP proxy payload
HTTPProxy.description: represents an HTTP or HTTPS proxy route used to mediate requests and responses under egress policy, credential scope, and disclosure controls.

InboundResponse.title: inbound response payload
InboundResponse.description: records an inbound response returned across a boundary, including origin, response data zone, status, tool or protocol context, injection scan result, and disclosure handling.

NetworkCall.title: network call payload
NetworkCall.description: records an attempted or completed network operation with destination, protocol, method, credential scope, proxy route, data zone, policy decision, and trace evidence.

network_call_id.title: network-call ID
network_call_id.description: Stable identifier for one actual network-interaction attempt.

method.title: method
method.description: Request method or operation name used by the network protocol.

started_at.title: started at
started_at.description: UTC instant at which the network call started.

status.title: call status
status.description: Whether the call was allowed, denied, succeeded, failed, or timed out.

NetworkPolicy.title: network policy payload
NetworkPolicy.description: defines allowed and denied network egress or ingress conditions, including destination host, port, protocol, method, proxy route, credential scope, data zone, and denial reason.

NetworkResource.title: network resource payload
NetworkResource.description: identifies a network-originated resource or endpoint whose content, credentials, response data zone, and trust boundary affect agent safety decisions.

OutboundRequest.title: outbound request payload
OutboundRequest.description: records a boundary-crossing outbound request from a tool, sandbox, protocol adapter, or remote-agent interaction, including target endpoint, method, credential scope, data zone, and policy decision.

ProcessPolicy.title: process policy payload
ProcessPolicy.description: defines process execution constraints such as command allowlist, argument limits, environment exposure, timeout, retry policy, resource limits, and sandbox escape handling.

Proxy.title: proxy endpoint payload
Proxy.description: represents a controlled proxy surface that mediates outbound or inbound traffic according to network policy, credential scope, logging, and data-zone constraints.

ProxyRoute.title: proxy route record payload
ProxyRoute.description: records the controlled proxy path used for network traffic, including proxy kind, target endpoint, allowed protocol, credential boundary, egress policy, and trace evidence.

Sandbox.title: sandbox payload
Sandbox.description: defines an isolated execution boundary that constrains process, filesystem, environment, network, credentials, and side effects for commands or tool calls.

SandboxEscapeRisk.title: sandbox escape risk payload
SandboxEscapeRisk.description: records evidence that a command, process, file operation, network route, or tool result may bypass or weaken the intended sandbox isolation boundary.

Socket.title: socket endpoint payload
Socket.description: records a communication endpoint used by sandboxed processes, proxies, tools, or network calls and links it to protocol, route, policy, and trace evidence.

SOCKSProxy.title: SOCKS proxy payload
SOCKSProxy.description: represents a SOCKS proxy route used to mediate network egress while preserving policy, target, credential, and audit metadata.

IsolationSpecification.title: isolation specification payload
IsolationSpecification.description: An IsolationSpecification is a versioned security specification with an applicable environment and enforcer. It states allow, deny, and monitoring constraints over processes, commands, paths, mounts, environment variables, network destinations, protocols, credentials, resource limits, and side effects. A concrete Sandbox references it through constrained_by.

NetworkEndpoint.title: network communication endpoint payload
NetworkEndpoint.description: A NetworkEndpoint is a communication entity with addressable identity, protocol, binding location, trust-boundary ownership, and applicable network policy. It may accept communication directly or forward traffic for a target. Proxies, sockets, and their protocol specializations qualify; requests and responses do not.

NetworkInteraction.title: network interaction payload
NetworkInteraction.description: A NetworkInteraction is an observable communication event with initiator, target endpoint, protocol, time, trust boundary, credential scope, policy decision, and outcome. NetworkCall and denied calls may specialize it, while OutboundRequest and InboundResponse are information carried by the interaction and connect through relations.

AuthorityScope.title: authority scope payload
AuthorityScope.description: defines the canonical authorization envelope for an actor, tool, service, or runtime by naming permitted operations, protected resources, data zones, trust boundaries, expiry, and enforcement limits.

BoundaryCrossing.title: boundary crossing payload
BoundaryCrossing.description: captures an observable transfer of control, data, artifact, message, or authority across a trust boundary, carrying source actor, target actor, source zone, target zone, direction, transferred object, authority basis, policy decision, protocol or tool context, and trace evidence.

crossing_id.title: boundary-crossing ID
crossing_id.description: Stable identifier for one identity-bearing, auditable trust-boundary crossing.

direction.title: crossing direction
direction.description: Flow direction of the payload or action relative to source and target zones.

purpose.title: crossing purpose
purpose.description: Explicit business purpose used to assess necessity and least authority.

occurred_at.title: occurred at
occurred_at.description: UTC instant at which the crossing actually occurred.

payload_digest.title: payload digest
payload_digest.description: Immutable digest of the crossing payload or action parameters for audit without disclosure.

DataZone.title: data zone payload
DataZone.description: groups data by trust, visibility, retention, and handling policy so that context, memory, tool output, and artifacts can carry boundary-aware controls.

ExternalBoundary.title: external boundary payload
ExternalBoundary.description: An ExternalBoundary is a TrustBoundary that separates the controlled agent runtime from outside organizations, services, users, or systems.

InternalBoundary.title: internal boundary payload
InternalBoundary.description: An InternalBoundary is a TrustBoundary that separates trusted runtime components, local stores, or internal services from less-trusted internal execution or data surfaces.

NetworkBoundary.title: network boundary payload
NetworkBoundary.description: A NetworkBoundary is a TrustBoundary at the transition between the runtime and network-accessible transports, proxies, resources, or remote services.

RemoteAgentBoundary.title: remote agent boundary payload
RemoteAgentBoundary.description: marks the opacity, identity, authority, and accountability trust boundary created when work, messages, tasks, or artifacts are delegated to or received from a remote agent.

ToolBoundary.title: tool boundary payload
ToolBoundary.description: A ToolBoundary is a TrustBoundary where model-controlled intent becomes an executable tool request or where tool output re-enters model-visible context.

TrustBoundary.title: trust boundary payload
TrustBoundary.description: defines a boundary where identity, authority, visibility, execution control, data handling, or accountability assumptions change and therefore must be explicit in safety decisions.

ToolTranscript.title: Tool Transcript payload
ToolTranscript.description: A ToolTranscript is a LogArtifact that preserves the ordered record of tool requests, permission prompts, ToolCallAttempt executions, results, warnings, and follow-up observations.

AuditLog.title: audit log payload
AuditLog.description: reviewable collection or channel of log records, audit references, telemetry summaries, and disclosure-relevant evidence prepared for inspection, diagnostics, or export.

ErrorListener.title: error listener payload
ErrorListener.description: subscribes to runtime, sandbox, tool, protocol, or log events in order to detect and route errors for diagnosis.

EventSink.title: event sink payload
EventSink.description: destination, consumer, receiver, or export target that accepts log streams, telemetry events, audit records, or trace exports for storage, monitoring, review, or forwarding.

LogListener.title: log listener payload
LogListener.description: observer component that subscribes to log streams or telemetry channels and routes records to diagnostics, review, metrics, audit logs, or event sinks.

LogRecord.title: log record payload
LogRecord.description: structured log record derived from a trace, span, event, diagnostic message, warning, error, tool result, or audit-relevant observation.

log_record_id.title: log-record ID
log_record_id.description: Stable identifier for one immutable log record.

timestamp.title: timestamp
timestamp.description: UTC instant at which the logged event occurred or was observed.

severity.title: severity
severity.description: Controlled severity of the log record.

body.title: body
body.description: Log body after applicable redaction has been applied.

LogStream.title: log stream payload
LogStream.description: ordered stream of log records or telemetry items delivered from runtime, tool, protocol, sandbox, or feedback components to listeners or sinks.

LogSubscription.title: log subscription payload
LogSubscription.description: subscription that registers a listener for a log stream, telemetry channel, audit feed, or diagnostic event source under filtering and retention conditions.

TelemetryEvent.title: telemetry event payload
TelemetryEvent.description: instrumented signal derived from runtime trace evidence and emitted for collection, monitoring, diagnostics, metrics, or export without redefining the raw TraceEvent.

TraceExport.title: trace export payload
TraceExport.description: exported trace package or portable trace artifact containing selected trace records, spans, events, attributes, redaction state, and provenance for debugging or review.

LogArtifact.title: Log Artifact payload
LogArtifact.description: LogArtifact is a provenance-bearing information artifact formed from runtime, trace, or diagnostic evidence; individual LogRecord, composite TraceExport, and migrated ToolTranscript can specialize it.

LogConsumer.title: Log Consumer payload
LogConsumer.description: LogConsumer is a role consuming LogStreams, LogArtifacts, or TelemetryEvents to listen, route, store, or forward; a subscription configures a consumer and is not itself a consumer.

CostMetric.title: cost metric payload
CostMetric.description: metric dimension measuring token, compute, money, call-count, storage, or operational cost for a run, task, tool call, route, or evaluation episode.

EvaluationCriterion.title: evaluation criterion payload
EvaluationCriterion.description: generic feedback-domain criterion that defines the scoring, success, rubric, or metric condition used to assess agent behavior while benchmark-specific criteria remain adapter mappings.

EvaluationRun.title: evaluation run payload
EvaluationRun.description: assessment episode that applies metrics, rubric criteria, success criteria, and scoring logic to observable agent behavior, run attempts, tasks, artifacts, or tool use.

evaluation_run_id.title: evaluation-run ID
evaluation_run_id.description: Stable identifier for one evaluation run over a pinned subject version.

started_at.title: started at
started_at.description: UTC instant at which the evaluation run started.

evaluator_id.title: evaluator ID
evaluator_id.description: Identifies the actor or system that performed or owned the evaluation.

status.title: evaluation status
status.description: Queued, active, completed, failed, or cancelled state of the evaluation run.

subject_id.title: subject ID
subject_id.description: Identifies the subject evaluated by this run.

subject_version.title: subject version
subject_version.description: Pins the immutable version or content digest of the evaluated subject.

EvaluationScenario.title: evaluation scenario payload
EvaluationScenario.description: generic evaluation setup describing task conditions, inputs, constraints, and expected observations while adapter-specific benchmark scenario fields remain outside feedback core.

LatencyMetric.title: latency metric payload
LatencyMetric.description: metric dimension measuring elapsed time, step latency, tool-call duration, queue delay, or response timing for observable agent work.

Metric.title: metric payload
Metric.description: measurable property of a run, task, tool call, route, artifact, policy decision, or feedback loop, expressed independently of benchmark-specific scoring schemes.

RobustnessMetric.title: robustness metric payload
RobustnessMetric.description: metric dimension measuring stability under retries, perturbations, long-horizon tasks, tool failures, context changes, or adversarial pressure.

Rubric.title: rubric payload
Rubric.description: generic scoring guide that defines criteria, weights, thresholds, and interpretation rules for evaluation without embedding benchmark-specific leaderboard semantics.

SafetyMetric.title: safety metric payload
SafetyMetric.description: metric dimension measuring policy compliance, risk exposure, unsafe output rate, boundary violations, or safety review outcomes without owning enforcement decisions.

Score.title: score payload
Score.description: numeric, ordinal, categorical, or pass-fail assessment value produced for a metric or rubric during an evaluation run.

confidence.title: Confidence
confidence.description: captures confidence as a number scalar on agent-system artifacts, resources, events, policies, or adapter records.

value.title: Score value
value.description: captures score as a number scalar on agent-system artifacts, resources, events, policies, or adapter records.

SuccessCriterion.title: success criterion payload
SuccessCriterion.description: condition used to decide whether a task, run, artifact, recovery attempt, or evaluation objective is acceptable under the relevant rubric or policy.

EvaluationSpecification.title: Evaluation Specification payload
EvaluationSpecification.description: EvaluationSpecification is a static, versionable specification used by evaluation runs, covering scenarios, rubrics, and criteria; it stores neither the evaluation execution nor observed measurements.

Measurement.title: Measurement payload
Measurement.description: Measurement is a provenance-bearing observation that references a Metric definition, measured subject, EvaluationRun, unit, value, time, and confidence; a Metric is reusable while every Measurement has independent identity.

confidence.title: Confidence
confidence.description: captures confidence as a number scalar on agent-system artifacts, resources, events, policies, or adapter records.

FeedbackEvent.title: feedback event payload
FeedbackEvent.description: observable control-loop event that returns critique, warning, preference, correction, approval, or revision signal to a task, route, worker, or optimizer.

AutomatedReview.title: automated review payload
AutomatedReview.description: machine-produced review pass over a task, output, plan, trace, or artifact that records findings, confidence, criteria, and provenance without replacing human approval when policy requires it.

Correction.title: correction payload
Correction.description: change proposed or applied to an artifact, plan, output, route, context assembly, or tool-use choice in response to feedback, diagnostics, failed validation, or review findings.

Feedback.title: feedback payload
Feedback.description: interpreted signal returned from review, diagnostics, metrics, user judgment, policy checks, or runtime evidence to improve, revise, block, approve, or reprioritize later work.

HumanReview.title: human review payload
HumanReview.description: review evidence supplied by a human actor, including approval, requested changes, blocking concerns, quality judgment, accountable reviewer identity, and reviewed object.

LearningSignal.title: learning signal payload
LearningSignal.description: feedback-derived evidence that may update memory preferences, retrieval configuration, routing policy, tool selection, policy thresholds, or future orchestration behavior.

OptimizationLoop.title: optimization loop payload
OptimizationLoop.description: bounded feedback loop that consumes feedback, metrics, scores, review findings, or learning signals to improve later routing, tool selection, prompts, policy thresholds, or task attempts.

OptimizationTarget.title: optimization target payload
OptimizationTarget.description: An optimization target specifies the property and direction to improve and is referenced by reviews and measurements.

RecoveryAction.title: recovery action payload
RecoveryAction.description: feedback-triggered or failure-triggered action such as repair, retry, reroute, stop, escalate, rollback, or resume that responds to a warning, denial, failed attempt, or review finding.

RecoveryPlan.title: recovery plan payload
RecoveryPlan.description: plan that selects one or more recovery actions, links them to the failure or feedback evidence that triggered them, and bounds retry, rollback, escalation, or stop behavior.

Review.title: review payload
Review.description: bounded inspection record over a task, artifact, trace segment, plan, answer, tool result, or policy-sensitive decision that can produce findings, approvals, requested changes, or feedback.

review_id.title: review ID
review_id.description: Stable identifier for the review record produced by one review activity.

subject_version.title: subject version
subject_version.description: Pins the immutable subject version examined by the review.

status.title: review status
status.description: Active, completed, withdrawn, or superseded state of the review record.

completed_at.title: completed at
completed_at.description: UTC instant at which review completed; null while active.

ReviewFinding.title: review finding payload
ReviewFinding.description: specific issue, observation, recommendation, defect, or approval note discovered during review and linked to the artifact, trace event, tool result, plan, or decision it concerns.

RollbackAction.title: rollback action payload
RollbackAction.description: action that reverses, compensates, or invalidates an unsafe side effect, state transition, artifact revision, memory update, route choice, or external operation under audit control.

ReviewActivity.title: Review Activity payload
ReviewActivity.description: ReviewActivity is an inspection with reviewer, assignment, subject version, criteria, and time bounds; it produces Review records and findings rather than being identical to them.

CorrectionActivity.title: Correction Activity payload
CorrectionActivity.description: CorrectionActivity is triggered by feedback or failure evidence and modifies, recovers, or rolls back an artifact, plan, state, or external effect; it is constrained by a plan and should be reevaluated.

BlockingError.title: blocking error payload
BlockingError.description: failure condition that blocks or terminates a run attempt, tool invocation, policy-gated operation, or protocol exchange until recovery, escalation, rollback, or operator intervention occurs.

ConfidenceSignal.title: confidence signal payload
ConfidenceSignal.description: confidence or uncertainty evidence attached to an observation, review finding, metric, retrieval result, answer, route choice, or policy-relevant decision.

DiagnosticMessage.title: diagnostic message payload
DiagnosticMessage.description: diagnostic output, debug message, explanation, or remediation hint derived from logs, trace events, validation failures, warnings, or review findings.

ErrorEvent.title: error event payload
ErrorEvent.description: observable event recording a failure, exception, timeout, validation error, blocked operation, or unrecoverable runtime condition with trace and diagnostic context.

error_code.title: error code
error_code.description: Machine-readable code for stable error classification and aggregation.

error_event_id.title: error-event ID
error_event_id.description: Stable identifier for one error event that actually occurred.

severity.title: severity
severity.description: Controlled level expressing the impact of the error.

retryable.title: retryable
retryable.description: Whether creating a new attempt under equivalent conditions is an allowed recovery strategy.

occurred_at.title: occurred at
occurred_at.description: UTC instant at which the error actually occurred.

ErrorStream.title: error stream payload
ErrorStream.description: ordered stream of error events, blocking failures, retryable failures, warning escalations, and diagnostic messages observed during runtime or protocol execution.

FailureMode.title: failure mode payload
FailureMode.description: classifies a recurring way an agent run, tool invocation, retrieval, policy gate, or protocol interaction can fail.

RetryableError.title: retryable error payload
RetryableError.description: identifies an error that may be retried under bounded policy because the failure is transient, recoverable, or externally caused.

RiskSignal.title: risk signal payload
RiskSignal.description: safety bridge signal recording evidence that an action, output, tool result, boundary crossing, instruction, or memory item may be policy-sensitive, unsafe, or trust-boundary relevant.

Warning.title: warning payload
Warning.description: non-fatal observable warning that records degraded confidence, policy concern, risky context, partial execution, or recoverable runtime issue without claiming final failure.

warning_code.title: Warning code
warning_code.description: captures warning code as a string scalar on agent-system artifacts, resources, events, policies, or adapter records.

WarningEvent.title: warning event payload
WarningEvent.description: observable event that raises a warning from runtime, tool, policy, retrieval, review, or telemetry evidence while execution may continue under constraints.

DiagnosticSignal.title: Diagnostic Signal payload
DiagnosticSignal.description: DiagnosticSignal is information attached to a run, attempt, result, or review to express warning, risk, confidence, or diagnosis; an event may emit it, but it is not the occurrence itself.

FailureEvent.title: Failure Event payload
FailureEvent.description: FailureEvent is an abnormal, failed, or warning occurrence at an explicit run, call, protocol, or policy location, with time, location, and impact; a failure mode classifies it and diagnostic signals explain it.

Chunk.title: chunk payload
Chunk.description: bounded segment of a document, output, message, or memory item with source span, boundary, provenance, and retrieval metadata used for indexing and context assembly.

chunk_id.title: chunk ID
chunk_id.description: Stable identifier for the chunk within a source-document version.

ordinal.title: ordinal
ordinal.description: Zero-based order of the chunk in its source document.

ChunkBoundary.title: chunk boundary payload
ChunkBoundary.description: structural boundary for a chunk, such as byte offset, token span, line range, page range, section break, semantic boundary, or overlap boundary.

ChunkCompression.title: chunk compression activity payload
ChunkCompression.description: The legacy 'compression artifact' wording conflates process and output; this is reviewed as an activity that preserves source anchors and produces a new Chunk, whose output remains a Chunk.

ChunkContextNote.title: chunk context note payload
ChunkContextNote.description: short note attached to a chunk that explains its local section, surrounding context, or reason for retrieval relevance.

ChunkingRun.title: chunking run payload
ChunkingRun.description: observable event that divides a document, output, or memory item into retrievable chunks with boundary, overlap, span, and provenance metadata.

ChunkOverlap.title: chunk overlap payload
ChunkOverlap.description: overlap span shared between adjacent chunks to preserve local continuity during retrieval and context assembly.

ChunkProvenance.title: chunk provenance payload
ChunkProvenance.description: provenance record linking a chunk to its document, source span, version, checksum, ingestion run, and transformation history.

ChunkQualitySignal.title: chunk quality signal payload
ChunkQualitySignal.description: quality signal for a chunk such as extraction confidence, parsing completeness, duplication status, staleness, toxicity, or retrieval usefulness.

ChunkReference.title: chunk reference payload
ChunkReference.description: reference from a chunk or retrieved chunk to the exact source span, anchor, line range, page range, offset, or document version it came from.

SituatedChunk.title: situated chunk payload
SituatedChunk.description: chunk augmented with local document context, section explanation, source purpose, or task-relevant grounding note before indexing.

chunk_id.title: chunk ID
chunk_id.description: Stable identifier for the chunk within a source-document version.

ordinal.title: ordinal
ordinal.description: Zero-based order of the chunk in its source document.

SituatingPromptRun.title: situating prompt run payload
SituatingPromptRun.description: observable event that adds document-local or task-local context to a chunk so retrieval can preserve section meaning, source purpose, and surrounding evidence.

ChunkArtifact.title: chunk artifact payload
ChunkArtifact.description: A ChunkArtifact is a provenance-, version-, and span-bearing information artifact that carries a Chunk, a locally grounded SituatedChunk, or a ChunkReference to an exact chunk and source span. It excludes chunking runs, boundary parameters, and standalone quality judgments.

ChunkMetadata.title: chunk metadata payload
ChunkMetadata.description: ChunkMetadata is auditable information attached to a Chunk or ChunkingRun with an explicit metadata kind, value or evidence, and provenance. It includes ChunkBoundary, ChunkOverlap, ChunkProvenance, ChunkQualitySignal, and ChunkContextNote. It is neither the Chunk itself nor an activity changing it.

ChunkProcessingActivity.title: chunk processing activity payload
ChunkProcessingActivity.description: A ChunkProcessingActivity is an activity with run identity, input version, strategy or model, output version, time, result, and error. It covers ChunkingRun, SituatingPromptRun, and ChunkCompression. Its products point to Chunk or SituatedChunk through produces; the activity itself is not an information artifact.

ContextAssembly.title: context assembly payload
ContextAssembly.description: ContextAssembly is an observable activity selecting, ordering, budgeting, and excluding information and producing a window and ContextPackage by relation.

assembly_id.title: context-assembly ID
assembly_id.description: Stable identifier for one context selection, ordering, and budgeting activity.

context_version.title: context version
context_version.description: Identifies the immutable context-snapshot version produced by this activity.

ContextBudget.title: context budget payload
ContextBudget.description: defines the token, window, cost, freshness, and priority limits used when deciding what information may enter a runtime context.

ContextExclusion.title: context exclusion decision payload
ContextExclusion.description: It records one decision to omit material for privacy, trust, relevance, safety, or budget and is not a subtype of a reusable rule.

ContextOrderingRule.title: context ordering rule payload
ContextOrderingRule.description: rule that orders context by priority, recency, freshness, authority, source trust, retrieval rank, task relevance, message role, and budget pressure.

ContextRefreshEvent.title: context refresh event payload
ContextRefreshEvent.description: observable update that reruns retrieval, replaces stale memory, recomputes summaries, or reorders context after new evidence, feedback, or state changes.

ContextSlot.title: context slot payload
ContextSlot.description: reserved position or segment in a context window for system instruction, task state, retrieved evidence, memory record, summary, tool result, or artifact.

ContextSource.title: context source payload
ContextSource.description: origin descriptor for context content, such as message, memory record, retrieval result, document chunk, tool result, trace observation, or artifact.

ContextSummary.title: context summary payload
ContextSummary.description: compressed representation of prior state, evidence, conversation, retrieved material, or memory records used to fit a context budget without storing hidden reasoning.

ContextWindow.title: context window payload
ContextWindow.description: bounded visible set of instructions, messages, retrieved chunks, memory records, summaries, tool results, and artifacts available to one model call or agent step.

ContextArtifact.title: context artifact payload
ContextArtifact.description: A ContextArtifact is information with context version, provenance, budget placement, and consumer association. It covers ContextWindow, ContextSummary, ContextSlot, and ContextSource. Final ContextPackage is uniquely owned by Info and referenced by relation; ContextAssembly and refresh are activities or events, and rules are excluded.

ContextRule.title: context rule payload
ContextRule.description: A ContextRule is a specification with identity, version, applicability scope, priority, and evaluation conditions. It covers ContextOrderingRule and ContextBudget. A ContextExclusion is a decision resulting from applying rules and is recorded by relation rather than subclassing the rule.

DenseVector.title: dense vector payload
DenseVector.description: dense embedding vector representation produced by an embedding model for semantic similarity search over memory records, chunks, or context candidates.

Embedding.title: embedding payload
Embedding.description: records a dense, sparse, lexical, or graph representation used by memory and retrieval indexes to compare or retrieve source material.

EmbeddingRun.title: embedding run payload
EmbeddingRun.description: observable event in which a specific embedding model and version encodes a chunk, document span, memory record, or query and produces an EmbeddingVector.

EmbeddingVector.title: embedding vector payload
EmbeddingVector.description: vector representation produced from a chunk, document span, memory record, or query for similarity search, ranking, clustering, or index construction.

GraphEmbedding.title: graph embedding payload
GraphEmbedding.description: records a graph-derived representation used to retrieve or rank graph nodes, edges, neighborhoods, or structured context candidates.

HybridIndex.title: hybrid index payload
HybridIndex.description: retrieval index that combines dense semantic vectors, sparse lexical features, or graph/context signals before scoring or rank fusion.

IndexBuildRun.title: index build run payload
IndexBuildRun.description: observable event that builds or refreshes an index from memory records, chunks, embeddings, lexical features, or graph links and emits an IndexVersion.

IndexEntry.title: index entry payload
IndexEntry.description: records an addressable item inside a retrieval index, including key, source reference, representation, and index-version metadata.

IndexKey.title: index key payload
IndexKey.description: records the key or lookup handle used to retrieve an index entry or candidate set from a memory or discovery index.

IndexRefreshEvent.title: index refresh event payload
IndexRefreshEvent.description: records an observable memory-index update, rebuild, or refresh event and the index version it produces.

IndexShard.title: index shard payload
IndexShard.description: partition of a vector, lexical, hybrid, or graph index used to scale retrieval while preserving index-version provenance.

IndexVersion.title: index version payload
IndexVersion.description: versioned snapshot of an index with build provenance, source coverage, embedding model, lexical configuration, shard layout, and validity period.

index_id.title: index ID
index_id.description: Stable identifier for the logical index across versions.

version.title: index version
version.description: Version value identifying one immutable index snapshot.

built_at.title: built at
built_at.description: UTC instant at which this index version finished building.

SparseVector.title: sparse vector payload
SparseVector.description: sparse vector representation such as weighted token, term, or feature dimensions used for lexical or hybrid retrieval over memory records and chunks.

TextEmbedding.title: text embedding payload
TextEmbedding.description: records a text-derived representation used by retrieval indexes for similarity search, ranking, clustering, or context selection.

TfIdfIndex.title: TF-IDF index payload
TfIdfIndex.description: lexical index that represents documents, chunks, or memory records through term frequency and inverse document frequency weights.

VectorDatabase.title: vector database payload
VectorDatabase.description: storage and query surface for vector indexes, embeddings, metadata filters, and nearest-neighbor retrieval over memory records or chunks.

VectorIndex.title: vector index payload
VectorIndex.description: index structure that organizes embedding vectors and metadata so retrieval queries can return similar memory records, chunks, or context candidates.

Representation.title: representation payload
Representation.description: A Representation is information with a representation method, generation provenance, version, and represented object. It may be an Embedding or another vector or structured representation. It is distinct from the activity generating it, the index hosting it, and the Chunk or MemoryRecord it represents.

VectorRepresentation.title: vector representation payload
VectorRepresentation.description: A VectorRepresentation is a Representation distinguished by vector dimensions, numeric type, sparsity, distance compatibility, and generation version. It covers DenseVector, SparseVector, and EmbeddingVector and is not a VectorIndex or VectorDatabase.

Index.title: index payload
