Index.description: An Index is a retrieval structure with index kind, configuration identity, input-representation requirements, and a version sequence. It covers VectorIndex, HybridIndex, and TfIdfIndex. Each build result is represented by IndexVersion, shards and entries connect through part_of, and backend databases connect through hosts.

IndexActivity.title: index activity payload
IndexActivity.description: An IndexActivity is an activity with run identity, source input, model or index configuration, output version, result, and error. It covers EmbeddingRun and IndexBuildRun. IndexRefreshEvent remains an independent event connected to versions through supersedes.

Database.title: database payload
Database.description: represents a database resource surface whose rows or query results may be referenced as context evidence through source references.

DatabaseRow.title: database row payload
DatabaseRow.description: represents a database row or query-result row that may be addressed through a source reference, subject to policy and provenance.

DatabaseTable.title: database table payload
DatabaseTable.description: represents a structured table within a database resource surface whose rows or slices may be referenced as context evidence.

DeduplicationEvent.title: deduplication event payload
DeduplicationEvent.description: observable ingestion event that detects duplicate or near-duplicate source material and links the retained record to the suppressed duplicate evidence.

DirectoryResource.title: directory resource payload
DirectoryResource.description: represents a directory-like resource that scopes files, paths, discovery, permissions, and traversal policy.

Document.title: document payload
Document.description: memory source artifact loaded or attached for parsing, metadata capture, chunking, indexing, citation, or later retrieval.

DocumentLoader.title: document loader payload
DocumentLoader.description: DocumentLoader is the capability to obtain resource content under access and trust controls; it relates to IngestionPolicy through use rather than a false common parent.

DocumentMetadata.title: document metadata payload
DocumentMetadata.description: metadata record describing a document's source, version, checksum, author, timestamp, format, permissions, trust zone, and parsing state for memory use.

FileResource.title: file resource payload
FileResource.description: represents a file-like resource with path, version, digest, permissions, and possible source spans available through a resource surface.

FileSystem.title: file system payload
FileSystem.description: represents a filesystem resource surface governed by runtime, safety, and access policy rather than a context input artifact.

GraphEdge.title: graph edge payload
GraphEdge.description: represents an addressable relationship in a graph store connecting graph nodes with labels, direction, provenance, and context relevance.

GraphNode.title: graph node payload
GraphNode.description: represents an addressable node in a graph store that may carry properties, labels, provenance, and context-relevant references.

GraphStore.title: graph store payload
GraphStore.description: represents a graph storage or graph retrieval surface whose nodes and edges may be referenced without making the store itself a context artifact.

IngestionPolicy.title: ingestion policy payload
IngestionPolicy.description: policy that decides which sources may be loaded, parsed, deduplicated, attached, stored, excluded, or sent for safety review before memory persistence.

IngestionRun.title: ingestion run payload
IngestionRun.description: An IngestionRun is an observable activity with inputs, loader, policy, result, and error, not a resource or artifact.

ingestion_run_id.title: ingestion-run ID
ingestion_run_id.description: Stable identifier for one source-to-memory ingestion run.

started_at.title: started at
started_at.description: UTC instant at which ingestion started.

SourceAttachment.title: source attachment payload
SourceAttachment.description: attached source object or reference carried into ingestion with provenance, location, checksum, and trust-boundary metadata.

StorageArea.title: storage area payload
StorageArea.description: represents a bounded storage surface or namespace governed by memory, runtime, access, and trust policies.

TextCorpus.title: text corpus payload
TextCorpus.description: represents a collection of text documents or text-bearing records prepared for ingestion, retrieval, citation, or context assembly.

IngestibleResource.title: ingestible resource payload
IngestibleResource.description: An IngestibleResource is an entity or information resource with stable identity or location plus access and trust context. An IngestionRun may read it through a DocumentLoader and transform it into a document, metadata, chunks, or memory records. It describes ingestion input only and excludes tool capability resources, runtime activities, and ingestion audit events.

MemoryAuditEvent.title: memory audit event payload
MemoryAuditEvent.description: observable audit event that records who read, wrote, updated, validated, deleted, exported, or reused a memory record.

MemoryCompaction.title: memory compaction payload
MemoryCompaction.description: operation that compresses memory content or history to reduce storage and context cost while preserving source anchors and retrieval utility.

operation_id.title: operation ID
operation_id.description: Stable identifier for one memory-lifecycle operation.

occurred_at.title: occurred at
occurred_at.description: UTC instant at which the operation took effect.

reason.title: operation reason
reason.description: Auditable reason for performing the lifecycle operation.

actor_id.title: actor ID
actor_id.description: Identifies the actor that initiated or performed the memory operation.

policy_id.title: policy ID
policy_id.description: Identifies the policy version authorizing and constraining the operation.

input_version.title: input version
input_version.description: Memory-record version read by the operation.

output_version.title: output version
output_version.description: New memory-record version produced by the operation; deletion is represented by a tombstone version.

result_status.title: result status
result_status.description: Controlled succeeded, failed, conflicted, or skipped result of the operation.

error_code.title: error code
error_code.description: Machine-readable error code when the operation fails; null on success.

MemoryConflict.title: memory conflict payload
MemoryConflict.description: record of contradiction between memory records, source evidence, preferences, policies, or current user instructions that requires resolution or supersession.

MemoryConsolidation.title: memory consolidation payload
MemoryConsolidation.description: observable operation that distills multiple observations, episodes, records, or feedback items into a summary, semantic memory, reflective memory, or procedure.

MemoryOperation.title: memory operation payload
MemoryOperation.description: A MemoryOperation is an activity with operation identity, actor, reason, applicable policy, input version, output version, time, result, and error. It covers write, update, merge, delete, discard, eviction, expiration, supersession, compaction, consolidation, validation, reflection, and decay. Every change creates a new version or explicit unavailable state and never rewrites old evidence in place.

CandidateChunk.title: candidate chunk payload
CandidateChunk.description: candidate chunk considered during retrieval and ranking, carrying source provenance, match evidence, score slots, and selection state.

CandidateSet.title: candidate set payload
CandidateSet.description: bounded set of retrieval candidates produced for a query before filtering, scoring, reranking, top-k selection, or context inclusion.

LexicalScore.title: lexical score payload
LexicalScore.description: lexical relevance score based on token, term, BM25, TF-IDF, or exact-match evidence between a query and candidate chunk or memory record.

RankFusion.title: rank fusion payload
RankFusion.description: ranking event that combines candidate lists or scores from multiple indexes, models, filters, or retrieval methods into a single ordering.

RerankScore.title: rerank score payload
RerankScore.description: score assigned by a reranker model or secondary scoring method after initial retrieval candidate generation.

RetrievalFilter.title: retrieval filter payload
RetrievalFilter.description: filter applied to retrieval by namespace, memory type, source, time range, trust zone, permission, freshness, task scope, or exclusion policy.

RetrievalQuery.title: retrieval query payload
RetrievalQuery.description: Query content, filters, budget, and index version form a replayable RetrievalArtifact; execution is represented by relations rather than conflating information with an event.

query_id.title: retrieval-query ID
query_id.description: Stable identifier for one replayable memory-retrieval query.

query_text.title: query text
query_text.description: Normalized query text submitted to retrieval.

top_k.title: top k
top_k.description: Maximum candidate count retained by the selection stage.

RetrievalTrace.title: retrieval trace payload
RetrievalTrace.description: trace record that explains query text, filters, index versions, candidate construction, scores, rank fusion, exclusions, and top-k selection.

RetrievedChunk.title: retrieved chunk payload
RetrievedChunk.description: chunk returned by retrieval with score, rank, source provenance, index version, and context-inclusion metadata.

SimilarityScore.title: similarity score payload
SimilarityScore.description: semantic similarity score between a query and candidate memory record or chunk, usually derived from embedding distance or vector similarity.

TopKSelection.title: top-k selection payload
TopKSelection.description: TopKSelection is a RankingOperation selecting candidates under k, budget, diversity, freshness, and policy constraints.

RetrievalArtifact.title: retrieval artifact payload
RetrievalArtifact.description: A RetrievalArtifact is an information artifact with query or run association, provenance, index version, and selection state. It covers RetrievalQuery, CandidateSet, CandidateChunk, RetrievedChunk, and RetrievalTrace. Scores are represented by RetrievalScore, filters by specifications, and RankFusion or TopKSelection by activities.

RankingOperation.title: ranking operation payload
RankingOperation.description: A RankingOperation is an activity with input CandidateSet, score evidence, policy, output ordering or selection, time, and result. It covers RankFusion and TopKSelection and excludes score values, candidate information, and the retrieval query itself.

MemoryFile.title: memory file payload
MemoryFile.description: file-backed persistent memory resource that preserves project conventions, user preferences, notes, plans, or cross-session context outside the immediate prompt window.

MemoryItem.title: memory item payload
MemoryItem.description: addressable memory unit that may be stored as text, summary, vector-backed chunk, experience, preference, procedure, or reflection.

MemoryNamespace.title: memory namespace payload
MemoryNamespace.description: isolation and lookup boundary that separates memory records by user, task, project, tenant, agent, thread, or cross-session reuse context.

MemoryRecord.title: memory record payload
MemoryRecord.description: persistent or session-scoped memory entry with content, provenance, type, namespace, scope, retention state, confidence, and lifecycle history.

memory_scope.title: memory scope
memory_scope.description: Composable session, task, or cross-session applicability scopes.

memory_kind.title: memory kind
memory_kind.description: Composable tags for episodic, semantic, procedural, reflective, preference, or experience kind.

memory_duration.title: Memory duration
memory_duration.description: Identifies a MemoryRecord's retention and use duration, such as working, short-term, or long-term; this dimension is independent of kind and scope.

memory_record_id.title: memory-record ID
memory_record_id.description: Stable identifier for the logical memory record across immutable versions.

version.title: version
version.description: Monotonic value identifying one immutable version of the memory record.

valid_from.title: valid from
valid_from.description: UTC instant from which this version is valid for reasoning or context assembly.

valid_until.title: valid until
valid_until.description: UTC instant after which this version is no longer valid; null for the current version.

confidence.title: confidence
confidence.description: Zero-to-one confidence assigned when the record was written or validated.

provenance_ref.title: provenance reference
provenance_ref.description: Points to the immutable source and location supporting the memory content.

access_policy_id.title: access-policy ID
access_policy_id.description: Identifies the policy governing read, update, and disclosure of this record.

MemoryRetentionPolicy.title: memory retention policy payload
MemoryRetentionPolicy.description: policy that governs how long memory records, summaries, indexes, and traces may be retained, refreshed, compacted, expired, deleted, or disclosed.

MemoryScope.title: memory scope payload
MemoryScope.description: policy boundary that states where memory may be read, written, updated, reused, disclosed, or forgotten across session, user, task, tenant, and trust contexts.

MemoryStore.title: memory store payload
MemoryStore.description: storage anchor that supports reading, writing, updating, deleting, merging, compacting, expiring, retrieving, and auditing memory records across session or long-term scopes.

MemoryEntity.title: memory entity payload
MemoryEntity.description: A MemoryEntity is an information entity with canonical identity, content or carrier, provenance, version, and lifecycle. It covers MemoryRecord, MemoryItem, and MemoryFile. Kind, duration, and scope are orthogonal record-field classifications rather than sibling pseudo-subclasses. MemoryStore, Namespace, Scope, and Policy govern it through relations.
